# Chores-11

Continuation of `chores-10.md` (which is closed at `0.50.0` —
the Subcommand trait sweep). This file covers the `0.51.0`
cycle onward. Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`README.md`](../README.md#reference-numbering); chores-11
starts at `[1]`.

## chore: move chores under notes/chores/ (0.51.0)

Commits: [[1]]

Single-step. `notes/` was dominated by ten chores files plus
the freshly opened `chores-11.md`; the eleven files now move
under `notes/chores/`, leaving `notes/` for the live notes
(`todo.md`, `done.md`, `README.md`, design docs). Filenames
stay `chores-NN.md` — preserving them keeps section-anchor
slugs unchanged and reduces the cross-file ref update to a
single mechanical substitution.

Done.md hygiene rides along: the eleven `0.44.0`–`0.50.0`
entries that had been sitting in `todo.md > ## Done` migrate
into `done.md` (renumbered `[85]`–`[95]`), since the 0.50.0
close-out marks a natural batching point and keeps `todo.md`
focused on what's actually in flight.

### Substitutions applied

External callers (everything outside `notes/chores/`):

- `/notes/chores-NN.md` → `/notes/chores/chores-NN.md` —
  absolute-path refs in `notes/todo.md`, `notes/done.md`,
  `notes/README.md`, the sibling design notes, `CLAUDE.md`,
  `ARCHITECTURE.md`, the root `README.md`, and the
  GitHub-URL fragments in `src/clone.rs` /
  `src/config.rs` / `src/init.rs` / `src/init/params.rs`
  / `src/push.rs` / `src/scope.rs` / `src/subcommand.rs` /
  `src/sync.rs`.

Within-chores siblings (now under `notes/chores/`):

- `(../ARCHITECTURE.md` → `(../../ARCHITECTURE.md` (the
  root `ARCHITECTURE.md` is two `..` away now).
- `(../src/…` → `(../../src/…`.
- `(README.md` → `(../README.md` (sibling
  `notes/README.md`).
- `(forks-multi-user.md` → `(../forks-multi-user.md` and
  the `[1]: forks-multi-user.md` ref def in
  `chores-08.md` (similar fixups for
  `vc-x1-init.md` / `cargo-locked-issue.md`).

In-chores cross-refs to other chores files (now siblings
in the same `chores/` dir) — `[chores-NN.md](chores-NN.md#…)`
style — are unchanged.

### Why this shape

`/notes/chores/` (under notes/) rather than top-level
`/chores/` keeps the notes umbrella intact —
`todo.md` / `done.md` / `README.md` are conceptually
adjacent to chores and benefit from sharing the
directory. The `chores-` filename prefix is technically
redundant once the files live in `chores/` (could become
`01.md` / `02.md` / …), but the rename was rejected to
keep the diff focused: section-anchor slugs are
filename-independent, so the rename would buy no
ref-resolution simplification and would force renumbering
of every `[N]:` URL slug in `todo.md`, `done.md`, and the
src `.rs` GitHub fragments.

## chore: open sb_ide elimination (0.52.0-0)

Commits: [[2]]

Multi-step. The `0.50.0` cycle landed every subcommand on
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch` with two trait peek methods
(`suppress_banner` / `is_detached_exec`) and one free
function (`sb_ide`) carrying per-run "session chrome" —
the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner, `finalize::surface_previous_failures`,
and the `bm_track` enter/exit pair. This cycle eliminates
all three by relocating each behavior to where it actually
belongs:

- The `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner already lands on `--help` via
  clap's `before_help`; printing it again at the top of
  every normal run is duplicate chatter the user can
  recover from `vc-x1 -V`. Drop the on-every-run banner.
- `finalize::surface_previous_failures` is a finalize-side
  concern; moving its trigger into `finalize` itself
  (rather than into a generic dispatch hook) cleans up the
  gate.
- `bm_track` enter/exit is a diagnostic; demoting it to
  `debug!` matches the "permanent sanity check, but quiet
  by default" intent already recorded in its module doc.

After these relocations the trait's `suppress_banner` /
`is_detached_exec` methods have no remaining consumers,
and `sb_ide` has no body left to keep — all three
disappear. Plan-only opener; the work lands in the
substeps below.

### What goes away — and the reasoning

- **`bm_track` info-level emission.** Currently emits
  `bm-track enter vc-x1 X: app(main)=tracked, …` on every
  run. The module doc already notes "a future refinement
  would remove the steady-state noise without losing
  detection value" — `debug!` accomplishes exactly that.
  Detection value is preserved (run with `-v` when
  investigating); steady-state output gets cleaner.
- **`sb_ide`'s banner-on-every-run.** `clap::Command::before_help`
  already shows the banner on `--help`. The on-every-run
  emission is duplicate; users who want it explicitly have
  `vc-x1 -V`. Removing it shrinks the default output by one
  line per command.
- **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` gated in `sb_ide`.**
  This is finalize machinery — it reads
  `.vc-x1/finalize-fail-*` markers and surfaces them. The
  "don't surface from the detached child" gate is
  finalize's concern, not the trait's; moving the trigger
  into `finalize` itself lets the gate live where the
  marker logic lives.
- **`SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec`.** With `bm_track`
  at `debug!` and `surface_previous_failures` relocated,
  no remaining consumer needs the trait's detached-exec
  peek. Drop the method; `finalize` reads `params.exec`
  directly as a finalize-private concern.
- **`SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner`.** With the banner
  gone from `sb_ide`, the `-L` flag still suppresses
  bookmark labels in the listing subcommands' output
  (chid/desc/list/show via `CommonParams::header`) — that
  already lives in the op layer, not the chrome layer.
  The trait peek has no remaining consumer; drop it,
  along with the `suppress_banner` field on `ChidParams`
  / `DescParams` / `ListParams` / `ShowParams`.

### Ladder

- 0.52.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo
  ladder (current)
- 0.52.0-1 `bm_track` → `debug!`; drop the bm_track gates
  in `SubcommandRunner::dispatch`
- 0.52.0-2..N eliminate `sb_ide`:
  - Drop banner-on-every-run from `sb_ide`
  - Move `finalize::surface_previous_failures` trigger
    into `finalize` itself
  - Delete `sb_ide` once both bodies have moved
- 0.52.0-K drop `SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec` and
  `SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner` (no remaining
  consumers); drop `suppress_banner` fields from the
  four `Params` types
- 0.52.0 close-out

### Per-step evaluation

Effectiveness is evaluated after each substep. Possible
outcomes at any step boundary:

- Continue as planned.
- Significantly modify the shape (e.g. keep the banner
  somewhere else; preserve `is_detached_exec` for a use
  case discovered mid-cycle) — recorded in a new chores
  subsection at the next step.
- Abandon: revert the cycle to a non-eliminated
  baseline, closing as a no-op with an `### Outcome` note
  capturing what didn't fit.

## refactor: bm_track → debug! (0.52.0-1)

Commits: [[3]]

First substep. `bm_track` enter/exit lines were
emitted at `log::info!`, which lit up on every
command run as `bm-track enter vc-x1 X: app(main)=tracked,
.claude(main)=tracked` plus its matching exit line.
The doc comment recorded the intent to silence the
steady-state noise "at which point a 'silent when
clean' refinement would remove the noise without
losing detection value" — `log::debug!` accomplishes
exactly that: default runs go quiet, `-v` brings the
signal back when investigating.

With the demotion, the `if !is_detached { bm_track(…) }`
gates inside `SubcommandRunner::dispatch` are
unnecessary — the detached `finalize --exec` child
runs at default verbosity, so debug-level output
silently no-ops in that path too. The gates collapse;
`is_detached_exec` survives the substep purely for
`sb_ide`'s remaining banner / `surface_previous_failures`
gate, which the next substeps will dismantle.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.52.0-0` → `0.52.0-1`.
- `src/main.rs`: `bm_track`'s two `log::info!` calls
  → `log::debug!`; module doc-comment reworded
  (drop the "future refinement" paragraph, note the
  new debug-level emission + why the gate isn't
  needed at the call site).
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `dispatch` drops the two `if
  !is_detached { crate::bm_track(…) }` gates around
  enter/exit; the local `let is_detached = …`
  binding disappears (its remaining consumer,
  `sb_ide`, reads the trait peek inline);
  doc-comment updated accordingly.
- `notes/chores/chores-11.md`: backfilled
  `Commits: [[2]]` on the 0.52.0-0 opener.
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.52.0-0 → `(done)`; 0.52.0-1
  marked `(current)`.

## feat: -V toggles version banner (0.52.0-2)

Commits: [[4]]

Second substep. `sb_ide` no longer emits the
`vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner on every command run, and `-V`
becomes a banner-toggle rather than print-and-exit:

- `vc-x1 chid -s code,bot` — runs silently (no banner).
- `vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -V` — prints
  `vc-x1 0.52.0-2` as the first line, then runs
  `chid` normally.
- `vc-x1 -V` — prints `vc-x1 0.52.0-2` and exits
  success (no subcommand to run).
- `vc-x1` (no flags, no subcommand) — prints help and
  exits non-zero (mirroring clap's old
  required-subcommand error path).

Replacing clap's auto-version means scripts can capture
the version *and* the command's output in one
invocation. The banner is the uniform `vc-x1 X.Y.Z`
regardless of subcommand — the version is the binary's
either way, and `propagate_version`'s
`vc-x1-<sub> X.Y.Z` form added noise without
information.

With the banner gone from the on-every-run path,
`sb_ide`'s `suppress_banner` parameter loses its only
consumer; the `SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner`
trait method and the `suppress_banner` field on the
four listing `Params` types (`ChidParams`,
`DescParams`, `ListParams`, `ShowParams`) cascade out
— they have no remaining callers once `sb_ide` stops
asking.

`-L` / `--no-label` still works — it suppresses the
per-repo header label in `chid` / `desc` / `list` /
`show` output, a concern that lives in the op layer
(`CommonParams::header`) and was always separate from
banner suppression. The trait peek was a duplicate
spelling of `-L` that survived only as long as
`sb_ide` asked for it.

`sb_ide` retains one remaining responsibility: gating
`finalize::surface_previous_failures` on
`!is_detached_exec`. The next substep relocates that
trigger into finalize itself, after which `sb_ide`
and `SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec` both
disappear.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.52.0-1` → `0.52.0-2`.
- `src/main.rs`: `Cli` drops `version,
  propagate_version = true` from `#[command(...)]`
  and adds a global `pub version: bool` arg (with
  `short = 'V'` / `long = "version"`);
  `command: Commands` → `command: Option<Commands>`;
  `fn main` emits the banner ahead of dispatch when
  `cli.version` is set, and handles the no-subcommand
  paths (print + exit on `-V`, print help + exit on
  no flags). `sb_ide` body drops the banner emission
  and its `if !suppress_banner` wrapper; signature
  drops the `suppress_banner: bool` parameter; doc
  rewritten.
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `dispatch` call updated to
  `crate::sb_ide(Self::is_detached_exec(&params))`;
  trait method `SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner`
  removed (no remaining consumer).
- `src/chid.rs` / `src/desc.rs` / `src/list.rs` /
  `src/show.rs`: `suppress_banner: bool` field on
  each `Params` struct removed; `TryFrom` impls drop
  the `suppress_banner: a.common.no_label,`
  assignment; trait `suppress_banner` overrides
  removed; doc-comments updated.
- `src/clone.rs` / `src/finalize.rs` / `src/init/tests.rs`:
  test helpers updated for the `Option<Commands>`
  shape (`match cli.command { Commands::X(a) => … }`
  → `Some(Commands::X(a)) => …`).
- `notes/chores/chores-11.md`: backfilled
  `Commits: [[3]]` on the 0.52.0-1 section.
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.52.0-1 → `(done)`; 0.52.0-2
  marked `(current)`.

## refactor: remove is_detached_exec from trait (0.52.0-3)

Commits: [[5]]

Third substep. The `SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec`
trait method goes away, taking `sb_ide` with it. The
detached-exec gate is finalize-specific machinery —
`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is the only
behavior it ever protected, and `FinalizeArgs.exec` is
the only field that ever set it. Lifting the gate into
`main` (one `matches!` against `Commands::Finalize`
with `args.exec`) cuts the trait peek + its sole
override + the free function that consumed it.

After this substep:

- The `SubcommandRunner` trait surface is the
  load-bearing minimum: `to_params`, `run`, and the
  default `dispatch`. No more peek methods.
- `main` owns the surface-previous-failures call,
  with the exec-child skip inline at the call site.
  Single home for the gate; no indirection through
  `sb_ide` or the trait.
- `dispatch` shrinks to "build params, bracket with
  `bm_track`, run, map exit code." No session-chrome
  responsibility at all.

The cycle's stated goal — eliminate `sb_ide` and both
trait peeks — is met after this substep. Close-out
lands separately as `0.52.0` (todo→done, no code).

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.52.0-2` → `0.52.0-3`.
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec`
  removed; `dispatch` drops its `crate::sb_ide(…)`
  call; doc rewritten.
- `src/finalize.rs`: `is_detached_exec` override on
  `FinalizeArgs` removed.
- `src/main.rs`: `pub fn sb_ide` deleted (no
  callers); `main` adds an inline
  `matches!(&cmd, Commands::Finalize(args) if args.exec)`
  peek and calls `finalize::surface_previous_failures`
  before loading `Context`.
- `notes/chores/chores-11.md`: backfilled
  `Commits: [[4]]` on the 0.52.0-2 section; new
  0.52.0-3 section.
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.52.0-2 → `(done)`; 0.52.0-3
  marked `(current)`.

## chore: close sb_ide elimination (0.52.0)

Cycle close-out. The `0.50.0` Subcommand trait sweep
left three pieces of per-run "session chrome" hanging
off `SubcommandRunner` — `sb_ide` (free function) and
the trait peek methods `suppress_banner` /
`is_detached_exec`. All three are gone after this
cycle:

- The on-every-run `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner is replaced by
  an opt-in `-V` flag that prints the banner as the
  first line and continues (rather than clap's
  exit-on-print default). Scripts can capture version
  *and* output in one invocation.
- `bm_track` enter/exit emit at `log::debug!` —
  default runs stay quiet, the signal is available
  under `-v`, and the detached `finalize --exec`
  child stays silent without needing a per-call gate.
- `finalize::surface_previous_failures` moved to a
  single inline call in `main` with an exec-child
  skip; `sb_ide` had no body left to keep and is
  deleted; the `is_detached_exec` trait peek (its
  only consumer) goes with it.
- `SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner` and the
  `suppress_banner: bool` field on the four listing
  `Params` types cascaded out during 0.52.0-2 when
  the banner emission left.

`SubcommandRunner`'s trait surface is now the
load-bearing minimum: `to_params`, `run`, and the
default `dispatch` (which builds params, brackets
`bm_track` enter/exit, runs, maps exit code). No
peek methods, no chrome responsibility.

### As-built ladder

- 0.52.0-0 plan + version bump + chores-11 opener
  section + todo ladder
- 0.52.0-1 `bm_track` → `debug!`; drop bm_track
  gates in `dispatch`
- 0.52.0-2 `-V` toggles version banner (replaces
  clap's auto-version); banner-on-every-run gone;
  `suppress_banner` trait method + `Params` fields
  cascade out
- 0.52.0-3 remove `is_detached_exec` from trait;
  gate moves to `main` inline; `sb_ide` deleted
- 0.52.0 close-out

### Outcome

Three subtractive substeps after the opener; no
evaluation-gate detours. The cycle's stated goal — a
trait whose surface only carries what every
subcommand actually needs — landed cleanly. The
`-V`-toggles-banner shape (instead of the original
"drop banner entirely" sketch) emerged mid-cycle when
the user pointed out that opting into the version on
demand is more useful than removing the signal
altogether.

While we were at it, the broader
`surface_previous_failures` design gaps (stale-forever
markers, concurrent surfacing double-print, mid-write
torn reads, no notify-at-failure path) got captured
as a new `## Bugs` section in `notes/todo.md`. The
exec-child gate covers one race; the rest is queued.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.52.0-3` → `0.52.0` (suffix
  dropped — cycle close marker).
- `notes/chores/chores-11.md`: backfilled
  `Commits: [[5]]` on the 0.52.0-3 section; new
  close-out section with the as-built ladder +
  outcome notes.
- `notes/todo.md`: deleted the In Progress ladder
  block; added `sb_ide elimination — banner off by
  default (-V toggles), bm_track → debug!, sb_ide +
  SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec,
  suppress_banner} removed (0.52.0)` to `## Done`
  with `[[2]]` ref.

1	# Todo

18	A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
19	highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
20	Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
21	in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
22	
23	Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
24	markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
25	renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
26	1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
27	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
28	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
29	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
30	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
31	   leaves to match: `scope`, `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`,
32	   `account`, `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
33	   (`init.rs`, tests).
34	1. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
35	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
36	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
37	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
38	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
39	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
40	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
41	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
42	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
43	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
44	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
45	1. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
46	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
47	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
48	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
49	   the felt bottleneck.
50	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
51	1. **Drop `-R`/`--repo` from `CommonArgs` once `-s`/`--scope` is
52	   established.**
53	   - `0.49.0-2.2` kept `-R` (single path) alongside the new `-s`
54	     (roles, composes with `-R` as workspace root) as a
55	     backwards-compat alias for the migration period.
56	   - Once users / scripts have moved, drop `-R` — `--scope` then
57	     covers both path and role forms (`parse_scope` already
58	     handles both; only `parse_scope_roles` would go away).
59	1. **`-s <path>` and `-s <path>,roles` workspace-root override.**
60	   - Today `-s` is keyword-only (`parse_scope_roles` rejects
61	     paths, pointing users at `-R`).
62	   - Future: have `parse_scope_roles` accept a comma list mixing
63	     one path + role keywords, where the path is the workspace
64	     root.
65	   - `vc-x1 chid -s ../foo,bot,code` would resolve to `[../foo,
66	     ../foo/.claude]` (today `-R ../foo -s code,bot`).
67	   - Decisions to make at design time: comma-list syntax, error
68	     cases (multiple paths, path without roles vs
69	     path-as-`Scope::Single`), and whether to also accept bare
70	     `-s <path>` as a synonym for `-R <path>`.
71	1. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
72	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
73	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
74	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)

85	1. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make

94	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side

173	   init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but

246	1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
247	1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility

251	1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
252	1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
253	1. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
254	   `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
255	   `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
256	   `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
257	   resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
258	   `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
259	   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
260	   `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
261	   Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
262	   project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
263	   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
264	   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
265	   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
266	   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
267	   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
268	   it adds value over the resolution chain.
269	
270	## Bugs
271	
272	Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
273	for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
274	and the cost of the failure. Same lazy `1.` numbering as
275	`## Todo` — markdown auto-numbers, reorder/insert without
276	renumbering.
277	
278	1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
279	   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
280	   four gaps:
281	   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
282	     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
283	     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
284	     are never surfaced.
285	   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
286	     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
287	     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
288	     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
289	     printed twice.
290	   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
291	     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
292	     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
293	     on write would close this.
294	   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
295	     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
296	     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
297	     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
298	     where there may be no next run.
299	
300	   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
301	   patches one related case — the detached child eating
302	   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
303	   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
304	   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
305	   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.
306

315	- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `scope`, `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`,
   `account`, `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).
1. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
1. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
1. **Drop `-R`/`--repo` from `CommonArgs` once `-s`/`--scope` is
   established.**
   - `0.49.0-2.2` kept `-R` (single path) alongside the new `-s`
     (roles, composes with `-R` as workspace root) as a
     backwards-compat alias for the migration period.
   - Once users / scripts have moved, drop `-R` — `--scope` then
     covers both path and role forms (`parse_scope` already
     handles both; only `parse_scope_roles` would go away).
1. **`-s <path>` and `-s <path>,roles` workspace-root override.**
   - Today `-s` is keyword-only (`parse_scope_roles` rejects
     paths, pointing users at `-R`).
   - Future: have `parse_scope_roles` accept a comma list mixing
     one path + role keywords, where the path is the workspace
     root.
   - `vc-x1 chid -s ../foo,bot,code` would resolve to `[../foo,
     ../foo/.claude]` (today `-R ../foo -s code,bot`).
   - Decisions to make at design time: comma-list syntax, error
     cases (multiple paths, path without roles vs
     path-as-`Scope::Single`), and whether to also accept bare
     `-s <path>` as a synonym for `-R <path>`.
1. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
1. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
1. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[4]]
1. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
1. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
1. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
1. **`--scope` sweep continuation: finalize + Single(_)
   dogfood.** Carry-over from 0.42.0 cycle (closed at -4.7;
   -5/-7 deferred). Two pieces: `vc-x1 finalize --scope`
   replacing `--repo` (was 0.42.0-5), and `Single(_)`
   end-to-end dogfood validation (was 0.42.0-7). The third
   originally-paired item, `vc-x1 clone --scope` (was
   0.42.0-6), is tracked in its own entry below. Design
   lives in chores-07. [[9]]
1. **init dual|por arg split.** Via `#[command(flatten)]` of
   `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle` (built in -6.7) +
   `provision_side(role, …)` shared helper. CLI surface
   decision (subcommands `init dual|por` vs preserved
   `--scope` flag with manual two-pass parse) deferred to
   design time. Was `0.41.1-6.9`; may or may not happen.
1. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
   `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
   the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
   across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
   coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
1. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
1. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
   `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
   `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
   forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
   single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
   or drop one spelling. [[10]]
1. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
   `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
   `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
   skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
   rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
   becomes scope-aware (single-side path skips
   `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`;
   `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
   close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
1. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
   (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
   `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
   Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
   `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
   `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
   Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
1. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
   once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
   override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
   init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
   post-init commands can. Depends on the
   `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
   Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
   `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
   `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
   under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
   Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [[12]]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
   resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
   matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
   per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
   Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
   `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [[20]]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
1. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
   `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
   `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
   `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
   resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
   `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
   (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
   `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
   Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
   project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
   it adds value over the resolution chain.

## Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
and the cost of the failure. Same lazy `1.` numbering as
`## Todo` — markdown auto-numbers, reorder/insert without
renumbering.

1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.
   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
     printed twice.
   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]

| **5** | `-s <path>` and `-s <path>,roles` workspace-root override | **Delete.** Same premise, same fate. |
| **12** | `--scope` sweep continuation: finalize + `Single(_)` dogfood | **Rewrite.** Two pieces: `finalize --scope` replacing `--repo` (still wanted, independent of scope refactor); `Single(_)` end-to-end dogfood (moot — `Single` variant is being deleted this cycle). Keep just the finalize piece. |
| **15** | `vc-x1 push: --scope=code\|bot\|code,bot\|<path>` | **Rewrite.** Drop `<path>` from the value list; push-side single-repo mode lives behind `-R` or whatever non-scope path mechanism survives. |
| **16** | `vc-x1 clone: --scope=code\|bot\|code,bot\|<path>` | **Rewrite.** Same — drop `<path>` from value list. (Clone's choice between dual and `por` becomes the new `--por` boolean, not a `--scope` form.) |
| **17** | `validate-desc / fix-desc: --scope=code\|bot\|code,bot` | **Light touch.** "`Single(_)` errors here" parenthetical becomes moot (no `Single` to error on). |

## Adjacent overlap — call needed

| # | Entry (short) | Question |
|---|---|---|
| **1** | single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field | The target list names `scope` as one of the leaves to sweep. `ScopeOption` is being deleted this cycle — drop `scope` from #1's target list? (Rest of the sweep is unaffected.) |
| **10** | Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema | Independent cycle (`0.54.0`); just add a note that scope cleanup lands first. Or leave the entry alone and let the next cycle's plan tie them together. |
| **14** | `init dual\|por` arg split | This entry frames the choice as "subcommands `init dual\|por` vs preserved `--scope` flag." Our `0.53.0` replaces `--scope` with a `--por` boolean — which is a *third* shape neither side of #14 considered. Two reads: (a) delete (the question is settled differently); (b) rewrite as "init: subcommands vs `--por` flag — keep `--por` for now, revisit if the binary gets richer." Which? |

## Sub-step proposal for 0.53.0-0

content.text:
You're right on the numbering — recounted, the correct mapping is **#13** (init dual|por), **#18** (push --scope), **#19** (clone --scope), **#20** (validate-desc/fix-desc --scope). My counts skipped over the four non-scope entries between #13 and #18. Apologies.

## On manual renumbering

The lazy `1.` convention was set explicitly in CLAUDE.md to keep insert/reorder friction-free, but the cost is real in conversations like this — neither of us can count without rendering. Three options:

1. **Keep lazy `1.`** — status quo. Maintenance cost zero; counting cost recurring.
2. **Manual `1.` `2.` `3.` …** — readable in source; every reorder/insert touches the numbering of every entry below. Diff noise is real on a reorder.
3. **Lazy `1.` in source + inline `<!-- #13 -->` marker per entry** — source readable, render unchanged, marker line is the only thing to renumber. Same cost as (2) when reordering, but the diff is cleaner (one marker line per touched entry, not the whole bullet).

The bot leans (2) — markers add a maintenance layer for visual readability the renderer already provides if you preview the file. (2) just makes the source match the render. Reorders are infrequent enough that the diff noise isn't a big tax. Worth doing as part of the 0.53.0-0 commit (a one-time renumber rides along with the cycle's todo edits), with a CLAUDE.md update noting the convention change. Or as its own micro-cycle if you'd rather keep 0.53.0-0 focused.

## Still need calls before 0.53.0-0 lands

1. **#13** (init `dual|por` arg split) — three options: (a) delete (settled by `--por` flag); (b) rewrite as "init: subcommands vs `--por` flag — `--por` chosen, revisit if richer needed"; (c) leave alone. Which?
2. **Cycle title.** `refactor: open scope cleanup (0.53.0-0)` — or different?
3. **Manual renumbering in this cycle, separate cycle, or skip?**

Once these are settled I'll TaskCreate the ladder and start `0.53.0-0`.

content.text:
Going with manual numbering — markers have lower long-term diff churn but worse source-readability, and reorders are infrequent enough that the manual diff cost is acceptable. The "literal number in front" wins for grepping/scanning.

Renumbering `notes/todo.md` now as the single edit of this turn. Updates: all 46 entries in `## Todo` get sequence numbers, the convention prose changes, the `## Bugs` prose changes, and continuation lines under 2-digit entries get the 4-space indent CommonMark needs.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Convert todo.md from lazy `1.` numbering to manual `N.` numbering.

- Renumbers all `1. ` entries in `## Todo` sequentially.
- Renumbers the (lone) `1. ` entry in `## Bugs` (stays `1.`).
- Fixes continuation indent (3→4 spaces) under 2-digit entries.
- Rewrites the convention prose in both sections.
"""
import sys

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

with open(PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    content = f.read()

OLD_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the\n"
    "markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering. Reference by displayed number (\"let's work on #3\")."
)
NEW_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is\n"
    "readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the\n"
    "entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being\n"
    "able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number\n"
    "(\"let's work on #3\"). Continuation lines under a two-digit\n"
    "entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3."
)
n = content.count(OLD_TODO_PROSE)
if n != 1:
    sys.exit(f"OLD_TODO_PROSE matched {n} times, expected 1")
content = content.replace(OLD_TODO_PROSE, NEW_TODO_PROSE)

OLD_BUGS_PROSE = (

file.content:
46. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.

## Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).

1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.

message.content: hmm, thinking about it, what if we add num-todo as a subcommand, I prefer rust code to over scripts, thoughts?

content.text:
Agreed in principle — Rust > shell/Python scripts inside vc-x1's wheelhouse. But I'd defer the actual subcommand to a future cycle, not bundle it into `0.53.0`. Reasons:

1. **Scope drift.** `0.53.0` is "scope cleanup"; adding a new subcommand with its own design surface (flags, dry-run/check mode, integration tests, doc comments) doubles the cycle's footprint.
2. **Design isn't trivial.** A real subcommand needs decisions: name (`num-todo` vs `fix-todo` vs `validate-todo`), file targeting (default `notes/todo.md` at workspace root?), sections it touches (`## Todo` + `## Bugs` only? or any `##` it discovers?), idempotency, check-vs-fix split paralleling `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`, error reporting, behavior when sections have already-correct numbering.
3. **The script's not pretending to be permanent.** It's there for *this* cycle's renumbering operations — including the upcoming deletions of #4, #5, #13, which will leave gaps the script needs to re-close. Once `0.53.0` closes, the Rust subcommand replaces it.

## Proposal

- **For this cycle:** keep `notes/renumber_todo.py` for the next few edits, generalize it slightly (match any leading number, not just `1. `, so it can re-close gaps after deletions), and delete it on `0.53.0` close-out as part of the docs/script sweep (`0.53.0-3`).
- **Add a new todo entry** — `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` subcommand. Parallels `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` shape. Goes near the existing `validate-repo` items so it lands as part of that broader "notes/repo hygiene" wave. Captures the work without committing to a cycle.

This way the Python script lives only as long as it has a job, and the Rust subcommand idea is on record where future-us will see it.

Does that match what you had in mind, or do you want the subcommand bumped to a higher priority?

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Renumber `notes/todo.md` entries in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`.

- Matches any leading `N. ` prefix (not only `1. `), so the script
  closes gaps after deletions and absorbs insertions equally well.
- Shifts continuation-line indent by the delta in prefix width
  (e.g. 4→3 when a 2-digit entry becomes 1-digit, or +1 the other
  way). Empty lines pass through.
- Idempotent. Convention prose is rewritten when the old form is
  still present; once converted, subsequent runs leave the prose
  alone. If neither old nor new prose matches, the script fails
  loudly rather than corrupting the file.

Slated for replacement by a Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`
subcommand pair in a future cycle (see notes/todo.md).
"""
import re
import sys

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

OLD_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the\n"
    "markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering. Reference by displayed number (\"let's work on #3\")."
)
NEW_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is\n"
    "readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the\n"
    "entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being\n"
    "able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number\n"
    "(\"let's work on #3\"). Continuation lines under a two-digit\n"
    "entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3."
)

OLD_BUGS_PROSE = (
    "Same lazy `1.` numbering as\n"
    "`## Todo` — markdown auto-numbers, reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering."
)
NEW_BUGS_PROSE = "Numbering convention same as `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …)."

NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def maybe_replace(content: str, old: str, new: str, label: str) -> str:
    """Replace `old` with `new` if present; tolerate already-migrated content."""
    if old in content:
        return content.replace(old, new)
    if new in content:
        return content
    sys.exit(f"{label}: neither old nor new prose found; manual fix needed")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []
    section: str | None = None
    entry_num = 0
    shift = 0

    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line.strip()
            entry_num = 0
            shift = 0
            out.append(line)
            continue

        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                old_prefix = m.group(0)
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix):])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue
            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)


def main() -> None:
    with open(PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = maybe_replace(content, OLD_TODO_PROSE, NEW_TODO_PROSE, "Todo prose")
    content = maybe_replace(content, OLD_BUGS_PROSE, NEW_BUGS_PROSE, "Bugs prose")
    content = renumber(content)
    with open(PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Renumbered todo.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Convert todo.md from lazy `1.` numbering to manual `N.` numbering.

- Renumbers all `1. ` entries in `## Todo` sequentially.
- Renumbers the (lone) `1. ` entry in `## Bugs` (stays `1.`).
- Fixes continuation indent (3→4 spaces) under 2-digit entries.
- Rewrites the convention prose in both sections.
"""
import sys

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

with open(PATH, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    content = f.read()

OLD_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the\n"
    "markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering. Reference by displayed number (\"let's work on #3\")."
)
NEW_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is\n"
    "readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the\n"
    "entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being\n"
    "able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number\n"
    "(\"let's work on #3\"). Continuation lines under a two-digit\n"
    "entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3."
)
n = content.count(OLD_TODO_PROSE)
if n != 1:
    sys.exit(f"OLD_TODO_PROSE matched {n} times, expected 1")
content = content.replace(OLD_TODO_PROSE, NEW_TODO_PROSE)

OLD_BUGS_PROSE = (
    "Same lazy `1.` numbering as\n"
    "`## Todo` — markdown auto-numbers, reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering."
)
NEW_BUGS_PROSE = (
    "Numbering convention same as `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …)."
)
n = content.count(OLD_BUGS_PROSE)
if n != 1:
    sys.exit(f"OLD_BUGS_PROSE matched {n} times, expected 1")
content = content.replace(OLD_BUGS_PROSE, NEW_BUGS_PROSE)

lines = content.split("\n")
out = []
section = None
entry_num = 0
two_digit = False

for line in lines:
    if line.startswith("## "):
        section = line.strip()
        entry_num = 0
        two_digit = False
        out.append(line)
        continue

    if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs") and line.startswith("1. "):
        entry_num += 1
        out.append(f"{entry_num}. " + line[3:])
        two_digit = entry_num >= 10
        continue

    if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs") and two_digit and line.startswith("   ") and (len(line) <= 3 or line[3] != " "):
        out.append(" " + line)
        continue

    out.append(line)

new_content = "\n".join(out)

with open(PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(new_content)

print(f"Renumbered {entry_num} entries in last section processed; file updated.")

Sorry — I clustered it next to #31 (the other validate-repo entry) for theme, but the file's convention is "ordered highest-priority first," so position implies priority, not theme. #32 isn't the right priority slot for a future-cycle hygiene tool.

Better options:

- **(a) Consolidate with the existing placeholders.** #15 is the one-liner "Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?" and #31 is the full design — arguably they're the same idea at different stages of capture. A `fix-todo` / `validate-todo` entry could fold into the same "notes/repo hygiene" placeholder, or pair with #15 as a sibling placeholder right next to it.
- **(b) End of list.** It's hygiene tooling for a future cycle; honest priority is low. Goes after #46 (Test-tempdir override).
- **(c) Stay near #31 (where I put it), but with an explicit note** that thematic adjacency overrides strict priority order here.

Where would you like it? The bot leans (a) — sibling next to #15 — because it captures the theme without inflating apparent priority. (b) is fine too; (c) feels like fighting the convention.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Renumber `notes/todo.md` entries in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`.
3	
4	- Matches any leading `N. ` prefix (not only `1. `), so the script
5	  closes gaps after deletions and absorbs insertions equally well.
6	- Shifts continuation-line indent by the delta in prefix width
7	  (e.g. 4→3 when a 2-digit entry becomes 1-digit, or +1 the other
8	  way). Empty lines pass through.
9	- Idempotent. Convention prose is rewritten when the old form is
10	  still present; once converted, subsequent runs leave the prose
11	  alone. If neither old nor new prose matches, the script fails
12	  loudly rather than corrupting the file.
13	
14	Slated for replacement by a Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`
15	subcommand pair in a future cycle (see notes/todo.md).
16	"""
17	import re
18	import sys
19	
20	PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"
21	
22	OLD_TODO_PROSE = (
23	    "Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the\n"
24	    "markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without\n"
25	    "renumbering. Reference by displayed number (\"let's work on #3\")."
26	)
27	NEW_TODO_PROSE = (
28	    "Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is\n"
29	    "readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the\n"
30	    "entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being\n"
31	    "able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number\n"
32	    "(\"let's work on #3\"). Continuation lines under a two-digit\n"
33	    "entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3."
34	)
35	
36	OLD_BUGS_PROSE = (
37	    "Same lazy `1.` numbering as\n"
38	    "`## Todo` — markdown auto-numbers, reorder/insert without\n"
39	    "renumbering."
40	)
41	NEW_BUGS_PROSE = "Numbering convention same as `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …)."
42	
43	NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")
44	
45	
46	def maybe_replace(content: str, old: str, new: str, label: str) -> str:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Renumber `notes/todo.md` entries in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`.

- Matches any leading `N. ` prefix (not only `1. `), so the script
  closes gaps after deletions and absorbs insertions equally well.
- Shifts continuation-line indent by the delta in prefix width
  (e.g. 4→3 when a 2-digit entry becomes 1-digit, or +1 the other
  way). Empty lines pass through.
- Idempotent. Convention prose is rewritten when the old form is
  still present; once converted, subsequent runs leave the prose
  alone. If neither old nor new prose matches, the script fails
  loudly rather than corrupting the file.

Slated for replacement by a Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`
subcommand pair in a future cycle (see notes/todo.md).
"""
import re
import sys

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

OLD_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the\n"
    "markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering. Reference by displayed number (\"let's work on #3\")."
)
NEW_TODO_PROSE = (
    "Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is\n"
    "readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the\n"
    "entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being\n"
    "able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number\n"
    "(\"let's work on #3\"). Continuation lines under a two-digit\n"
    "entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3."
)

OLD_BUGS_PROSE = (
    "Same lazy `1.` numbering as\n"
    "`## Todo` — markdown auto-numbers, reorder/insert without\n"
    "renumbering."
)
NEW_BUGS_PROSE = "Numbering convention same as `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …)."

NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def maybe_replace(content: str, old: str, new: str, label: str) -> str:
    """Replace `old` with `new` if present; tolerate already-migrated content."""
    if old in content:
        return content.replace(old, new)
    if new in content:
        return content
    sys.exit(f"{label}: neither old nor new prose found; manual fix needed")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []
    section: str | None = None
    entry_num = 0
    shift = 0

    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line.strip()
            entry_num = 0
            shift = 0
            out.append(line)
            continue

        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                old_prefix = m.group(0)
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix):])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue
            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)


def main() -> None:
    with open(PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = maybe_replace(content, OLD_TODO_PROSE, NEW_TODO_PROSE, "Todo prose")
    content = maybe_replace(content, OLD_BUGS_PROSE, NEW_BUGS_PROSE, "Bugs prose")
    content = renumber(content)
    with open(PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Renumbered todo.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
Before I do the next mechanical work, I need a call on substep boundary. The current state isn't yet a coherent `0.53.0-0` commit — the planned `0.53.0-0` opener also needs:

1. Backfill `chores-11.md` `Commits:` ref for 0.52.0 (`9ba2b67...`).
2. Bump `Cargo.toml` `0.52.0` → `0.53.0-0`.
3. Add new `chores-11.md` section for the cycle plan.
4. Add `## In Progress` ladder in `todo.md`.
5. Apply the planned todo edits — delete #5/#6/#14, rewrite #13/#19/#20/#21, drop `scope` from #2's target list.
6. Pre-commit cargo cycle.

Two ways to interpret "commit substeps as we complete them":

**Option A — single `0.53.0-0` commit.** Finish all the above, then one commit containing the tooling work + plan + bump + chores + todo edits. `0.53.0-1`, `-2`, `-3`, close-out each get their own commits as planned. Total: 5 commits.

**Option B — sub-sub-step split of `0.53.0-0`.**
- `0.53.0-0.1`: bump to `0.53.0-0.1` + chores opener section + In Progress ladder + backfill 0.52.0 + the tooling work we just did.
- `0.53.0-0.2`: todo edits (deletes + rewrites + drop `scope`).
- `0.53.0-0`: close the sub-sub-step (squashed or kept).
- Then `0.53.0-1`, `-2`, `-3`, close.

The bot leans **A** — the work currently in flight is small enough that splitting `0.53.0-0` into two sub-sub-steps adds ladder ceremony without much payoff. The tooling change (renumber + script + new entry) is preparatory infrastructure for the cycle's todo edits, so it naturally belongs in the same opener.

Which do you want?

## refactor: clean up scope + todo list (0.53.0-0)

Multi-step. Two `--scope` flags coexist with different
grammars: query-side `-s code|bot|code,bot` (which sides of
an existing workspace) and creation-side `-s code,bot|por`
(workspace topology). Same spelling, different parsers,
different types — the bot ran into them while planning and
miscounted todo entries in the process. The cleanup factors
the three concerns apart:

- **Path** stays on `-R/--repo`. Always present (defaults to
  cwd). Independent of roles.
- **Roles** stay on `-s/--scope`
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`). Single-purpose: which
  side(s) of an existing dual workspace.
- **Topology** moves to a boolean `--por` flag at creation
  time (init, clone). The `code,bot|por` grammar collapses
  to "do you want a `.claude` companion or not."

After this cycle:

- `Scope::Single(PathBuf)` — gone. `-R <path>` is the
  path-only entry point; the `Scope` runtime type holds
  only roles.
- `parse_scope_roles` — gone. Its job (reject path forms in
  the roles slot) is moot once paths aren't a roles form.
- `options_flags/scope.rs` (`ScopeKind`, `ScopeOption`,
  `parse_scope_kind`, `ScopeParser`) — replaced by a
  `PorFlag` leaf in `options_flags/`.

The deeper symmetric-`.vc-config.toml` refactor (todo #11)
is independent and lands in `0.54.0`; keeping it separate
keeps each cycle reviewable.

### Todo items the cycle absorbs

Deletes:

- #5 (was #4): "Drop `-R/--repo` from `CommonArgs`" —
  premised on folding `<path>` into `-s`, rejected here.
- #6 (was #5): "`-s <path>` workspace-root override" —
  same premise, same fate.
- #14 (was #13): "init dual|por arg split" — settled
  with `--por` flag (a third shape neither half of the
  entry considered).

Rewrites / light touch:

- #2 (was #1): drop `scope` from the single-field
  leaves sweep — `ScopeOption` is being deleted.
- #13 (was #12): `--scope` sweep continuation —
  `Single(_)` dogfood half drops out; only the
  `finalize --scope` replacing `--repo` half survives.
- #19 (was #18), #20 (was #19): push/clone
  `--scope=...|<path>` flag designs — `<path>` form
  drops out.
- #21 (was #20): validate-desc/fix-desc `--scope` —
  `Single(_)`-errors-here parenthetical moot.

A new entry #1 (`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`)
captures the future Rust replacement for the interim
`notes/fix-todo.py`.

### Notes-side prep done at -0

The cycle's todo edits needed clean ordinal references, so
the opener also lands a notes-convention change:

- `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entries now use manual numbering
  (`1.` `2.` …) rather than lazy `1.` everywhere. The raw
  source is readable without rendering, at the cost of
  diff churn on reorder. See the convention prose in
  todo.md.
- `notes/fix-todo.py` — interim Python script that
  renumbers and shifts continuation indent on a delete /
  reorder. Slated for Rust replacement (todo #1).

### Ladder

- 0.53.0-0 plan + version bump + notes convention +
  fix-todo script + todo edits (current)
- 0.53.0-1 collapse `src/scope.rs` to roles-only —
  drop `Scope::Single`, `parse_scope_roles`; simplify
  parser + helpers
- 0.53.0-2 replace `ScopeKind`/`ScopeOption` with
  `--por` flag — delete `options_flags/scope.rs`;
  migrate init / clone / test_helpers
- 0.53.0-3 docs/script sweep — README, ARCHITECTURE,
  substep-test.sh, notes references to the old
  `--scope code,bot|por` form; delete `notes/fix-todo.py`
- 0.53.0 close-out

36	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
37	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
38	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
39	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
40	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
41	   leaves to match: `scope`, `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`,
42	   `account`, `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
43	   (`init.rs`, tests).
44	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
45	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
46	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
47	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
48	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
49	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
50	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
51	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
52	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
53	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
54	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
55	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
56	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
57	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
58	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
59	   the felt bottleneck.
60	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
61	5. **Drop `-R`/`--repo` from `CommonArgs` once `-s`/`--scope` is
62	   established.**
63	   - `0.49.0-2.2` kept `-R` (single path) alongside the new `-s`
64	     (roles, composes with `-R` as workspace root) as a
65	     backwards-compat alias for the migration period.
66	   - Once users / scripts have moved, drop `-R` — `--scope` then
67	     covers both path and role forms (`parse_scope` already
68	     handles both; only `parse_scope_roles` would go away).
69	6. **`-s <path>` and `-s <path>,roles` workspace-root override.**
70	   - Today `-s` is keyword-only (`parse_scope_roles` rejects
71	     paths, pointing users at `-R`).
72	   - Future: have `parse_scope_roles` accept a comma list mixing
73	     one path + role keywords, where the path is the workspace
74	     root.
75	   - `vc-x1 chid -s ../foo,bot,code` would resolve to `[../foo,
76	     ../foo/.claude]` (today `-R ../foo -s code,bot`).
77	   - Decisions to make at design time: comma-list syntax, error
78	     cases (multiple paths, path without roles vs
79	     path-as-`Scope::Single`), and whether to also accept bare
80	     `-s <path>` as a synonym for `-R <path>`.

file.content:
2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `scope`, `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`,
   `account`, `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).
3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
5. **Drop `-R`/`--repo` from `CommonArgs` once `-s`/`--scope` is
   established.**
   - `0.49.0-2.2` kept `-R` (single path) alongside the new `-s`
     (roles, composes with `-R` as workspace root) as a
     backwards-compat alias for the migration period.
   - Once users / scripts have moved, drop `-R` — `--scope` then
     covers both path and role forms (`parse_scope` already
     handles both; only `parse_scope_roles` would go away).
6. **`-s <path>` and `-s <path>,roles` workspace-root override.**
   - Today `-s` is keyword-only (`parse_scope_roles` rejects
     paths, pointing users at `-R`).
   - Future: have `parse_scope_roles` accept a comma list mixing
     one path + role keywords, where the path is the workspace
     root.
   - `vc-x1 chid -s ../foo,bot,code` would resolve to `[../foo,
     ../foo/.claude]` (today `-R ../foo -s code,bot`).
   - Decisions to make at design time: comma-list syntax, error
     cases (multiple paths, path without roles vs
     path-as-`Scope::Single`), and whether to also accept bare
     `-s <path>` as a synonym for `-R <path>`.

content.subject: 0.53.0-0: plan + version bump + todo cleanup
content.description: Cycle opener. Renumber todo.md (done), backfill chores-11 Commits ref for 0.52.0, delete todos #4/#5/#13, rewrite #12/#18/#19/#20 light touch, drop `scope` from #1's target list, add In Progress ladder, bump Cargo.toml 0.52.0 → 0.53.0-0, add chores-11 section "refactor: clean up scope + todo list (0.53.0-0)", run pre-commit cargo cycle.

content.subject: 0.53.0-1: collapse src/scope.rs to roles-only
content.description: Drop `Scope::Single(PathBuf)`, drop `parse_scope_roles` (folds into `parse_scope`), simplify the grammar to `code|bot|code,bot|bot,code` only. Update `CommonArgs`, `sync`, `common.rs`, `init` runtime call sites. Tests get the same trim.

content.subject: 0.53.0-2: replace ScopeKind/ScopeOption with --por flag
content.description: Delete `options_flags/scope.rs` (ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind, ScopeParser, ScopeOption). Add a `PorFlag` leaf in `options_flags/` carrying a single boolean. Migrate `init`, `clone`, `test_helpers`, and `init/params.rs` to consume the boolean. Help text: "Create a single plain repo (no .claude/ companion)."

content.subject: 0.53.0-3: docs/script sweep
content.description: Update README.md, notes/substep-test.sh, ARCHITECTURE.md, and any chores/notes references that mention the old `--scope code,bot|por` form. Also notes/README.md if it documents the convention.

content.subject: 0.53.0: close-out
content.description: Final commit dropping the `-N` suffix. notes/todo.md: move In Progress entry to ## Done with [N] ref; update notes/chores/chores-11.md with close-out section + as-built ladder + outcome notes; backfill 0.53.0-3's Commits ref; ensure chores section headers match commit titles. Pre-commit cargo cycle, then push.

   - `0.49.0-2.2` kept `-R` (single path) alongside the new `-s`

     covers both path and role forms (`parse_scope` already

6. **`-s <path>` and `-s <path>,roles` workspace-root override.**
   - Today `-s` is keyword-only (`parse_scope_roles` rejects
     paths, pointing users at `-R`).
   - Future: have `parse_scope_roles` accept a comma list mixing
     one path + role keywords, where the path is the workspace
     root.

     ../foo/.claude]` (today `-R ../foo -s code,bot`).
   - Decisions to make at design time: comma-list syntax, error
     cases (multiple paths, path without roles vs
     path-as-`Scope::Single`), and whether to also accept bare
     `-s <path>` as a synonym for `-R <path>`.
