1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	## In Progress
7	
8	A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
9	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
10	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
11	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
12	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
13	
14	   - 0.53.0-0 plan + version bump + notes convention + fix-todo script + todo edits (done)
15	   - 0.53.0-1 collapse `src/scope.rs` to roles-only
16	   - 0.53.0-2 replace `ScopeKind`/`ScopeOption` with `--por` flag
17	   - 0.53.0-3 docs/script sweep + delete `notes/fix-todo.py`
18	   - 0.53.0 close-out
19	
20	## Todo
21	
22	A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
23	highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
24	Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
25	in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
26	
27	Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is
28	readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the
29	entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being
30	able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number
31	("let's work on #3"). Continuation lines under a two-digit
32	entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3.
33	1. **`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` subcommands.** Parallel
34	   shape to `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` (check vs rewrite).
35	   Renumber `## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries in `notes/todo.md`,
36	   normalize continuation-line indent to match prefix width.
37	   Rust replacement for the interim `notes/fix-todo.py`.
38	   Belongs in the broader "notes/repo hygiene" wave alongside
39	   `validate-repo` ([[19]]).
40	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
41	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
42	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
43	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
44	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
45	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
46	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
47	   (`init.rs`, tests).
48	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
49	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
50	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
51	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
52	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
53	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
54	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
55	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
56	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
57	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
58	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
59	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
60	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
61	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
62	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
63	   the felt bottleneck.
64	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
65	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
66	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
67	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
68	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
69	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
70	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
71	6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
72	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
73	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
74	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
75	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
76	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
77	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
78	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
79	7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
80	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
81	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
82	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
83	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
84	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
85	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
86	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
87	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
88	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
89	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
90	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
91	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
92	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
93	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
94	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
95	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
96	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
97	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
98	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
99	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
100	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
101	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
102	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
103	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
104	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
105	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
106	    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
107	    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
108	    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
109	    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
110	11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
111	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
112	    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
113	    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
114	    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
115	    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
116	    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
117	12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
118	    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
119	    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
120	    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
121	    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
122	13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
123	14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
124	    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
125	    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
126	    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
127	    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
128	    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
129	15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
130	    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
131	    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
132	    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
133	16. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
134	    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
135	    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
136	    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
137	    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
138	    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
139	17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
140	    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
141	    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
142	    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
143	    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
144	18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
145	    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
146	    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
147	    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
148	    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
149	19. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
150	    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
151	    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
152	    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
153	    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
154	    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.

content.subject: 0.53.0-0: plan + version bump + todo cleanup
content.description: Cycle opener. Renumber todo.md (done), backfill chores-11 Commits ref for 0.52.0, delete todos #4/#5/#13, rewrite #12/#18/#19/#20 light touch, drop `scope` from #1's target list, add In Progress ladder, bump Cargo.toml 0.52.0 → 0.53.0-0, add chores-11 section "refactor: clean up scope + todo list (0.53.0-0)", run pre-commit cargo cycle.

content.subject: 0.53.0-1: collapse src/scope.rs to roles-only
content.description: Drop `Scope::Single(PathBuf)`, drop `parse_scope_roles` (folds into `parse_scope`), simplify the grammar to `code|bot|code,bot|bot,code` only. Update `CommonArgs`, `sync`, `common.rs`, `init` runtime call sites. Tests get the same trim.

content.subject: 0.53.0-2: replace ScopeKind/ScopeOption with --por flag
content.description: Delete `options_flags/scope.rs` (ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind, ScopeParser, ScopeOption). Add a `PorFlag` leaf in `options_flags/` carrying a single boolean. Migrate `init`, `clone`, `test_helpers`, and `init/params.rs` to consume the boolean. Help text: "Create a single plain repo (no .claude/ companion)."

content.subject: 0.53.0-3: docs/script sweep
content.description: Update README.md, notes/substep-test.sh, ARCHITECTURE.md, and any chores/notes references that mention the old `--scope code,bot|por` form. Also notes/README.md if it documents the convention.

content.subject: 0.53.0: close-out
content.description: Final commit dropping the `-N` suffix. notes/todo.md: move In Progress entry to ## Done with [N] ref; update notes/chores/chores-11.md with close-out section + as-built ladder + outcome notes; backfill 0.53.0-3's Commits ref; ensure chores section headers match commit titles. Pre-commit cargo cycle, then push.

content.text:
todo.md edits done. From the diff: 47 entries before, 44 after (3 deletes), rewrites all in place, target list in #2 no longer mentions `scope`. In Progress ladder is now at lines 14-18, 3-space indented to match the format example above it.

**This unit's changes:**
- `notes/todo.md`:
  - #2 target list: dropped `scope`.
  - #5 (was "Drop -R/--repo from CommonArgs") deleted.
  - #6 (was "-s <path> workspace-root override") deleted.
  - #14 (was "init dual|por arg split") deleted.
  - #13 (was "--scope sweep continuation: finalize + Single(_) dogfood") rewritten as #11 "`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag" — drops Single(_) half.
  - #19, #20 ("push/clone --scope=...|<path>") rewritten as #16, #17 — drops `<path>` from value list.
  - #21 (validate-desc/fix-desc) rewritten as #18 — drops the `Single(_)` errors-here parenthetical.
  - `_No cycle currently in progress._` replaced with the cycle ladder (5 bullets, 0.53.0-0 marked `(done)` per the end-of-substep flip rule).
- Ran `fix-todo.py` to compact numbering after deletes.

Stopping for review. Remaining `0.53.0-0` work: pre-commit cargo cycle (fmt + clippy + test + install --locked + retest), then I propose the commit message.

continue commit cycle

input.old_string:
NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []
    section: str | None = None
    entry_num = 0
    shift = 0

    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line.strip()
            entry_num = 0
            shift = 0
            out.append(line)
            continue

        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                old_prefix = m.group(0)
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix):])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue
            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)
input.new_string:
# Regex for a leading `N. ` on a line — `\d+` is one or more digits,
# `\.` is a literal dot, `\s` is a whitespace character.
NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    # Split the file into lines, walk them, build the new file line-by-line
    # in `out`, then rejoin at the end.
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []

    # State carried across the loop:
    section: str | None = None  # current `## Heading`, or None outside any
    entry_num = 0               # running entry count within `section`
    shift = 0                   # for the most-recent entry's continuation
                                # lines: how many spaces to add (positive)
                                # or remove (negative) from their indent so
                                # they stay aligned under the new prefix.

    for line in lines:
        # Section heading — entering a new section; reset counters.
        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line.strip()
            entry_num = 0
            shift = 0
            out.append(line)
            continue

        # Only the two numbered sections need renumbering.
        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            # Entry first line: starts with `N. `. Renumber to the next
            # sequential value and record the prefix-width delta for the
            # entry's continuation lines that follow.
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                old_prefix = m.group(0)            # e.g. "9. "  or "10. "
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "      # e.g. "10. " or "9. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix):])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue

            # Continuation line under the current entry. If the entry's
            # prefix changed width (shift != 0), pad or trim the leading
            # spaces by the same amount so the text stays aligned under
            # the new prefix. Blank lines pass through (no indent to fix).
            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        # Everything else (blank lines, lines outside the two numbered
        # sections, continuation lines when shift == 0) passes through
        # unchanged.
        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Renumber `notes/todo.md` entries in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`.

- Matches any leading `N. ` prefix (not only `1. `), so the script
  closes gaps after deletions and absorbs insertions equally well.
- Shifts continuation-line indent by the delta in prefix width
  (e.g. 4→3 when a 2-digit entry becomes 1-digit, or +1 the other
  way). Empty lines pass through.
- Idempotent on already-renumbered content.

Slated for replacement by a Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`
subcommand pair in a future cycle (see notes/todo.md).
"""
import re

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []
    section: str | None = None
    entry_num = 0
    shift = 0

    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line.strip()
            entry_num = 0
            shift = 0
            out.append(line)
            continue

        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                old_prefix = m.group(0)
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix):])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue
            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)


def main() -> None:
    with open(PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = renumber(content)
    with open(PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Renumbered todo.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
**Line 52-53 — `out.append(line)` vs `out.append(section)`**: they only differ when a heading has trailing whitespace (`line` keeps it, `section` strips it). Today's todo.md has no trailing-space headings, so it's a non-issue in practice. The bot leans `line` though, because the script's job is renumbering — touching unrelated whitespace is a separate concern (and a better fit for a future `validate-todo` that audits whole-file hygiene). Preserve, don't silently normalize. So: keep `line`.

**Line 72 — "Assumes no empty lines between numbered items"**: no, blank lines are handled. A blank line fails the `line.strip() != ""` guard, falls through past the continuation branch, and lands on the unconditional `out.append(line)` at the bottom of the loop — passed through unchanged. `shift` carries forward across the blank line and is reset only by the next entry line (which sets a new `shift`) or the next `## ` heading. So:

```
9. **foo**           ← sets shift = +1 (was 8.)
   continuation      ← shift in effect → 3 spaces → 4 spaces
                     ← blank, passes through
10. **bar**          ← shift recomputed for this entry
```

Works correctly.

Want me to strip the `(?)` comments (and fix the `calcuated`/`Number start with` typos in your annotations) before we commit, or leave the questions/typos in as inline reading notes?

Migrate `init`, `clone`, `test_helpers`, and `init/params.rs` to consume the boolean. Help text: "Create a single plain repo (no .claude/ companion)."

content.text:
Good catch. The script's `^(\d+)\.\s` only knows "starts with a digit at column 0" — anything else in the section that happens to fit that shape would match too. The fragility is real, even if today's prose dodges it by luck.

Indenting intro prose by 1 space is the clean fix: CommonMark strips up to 3 leading spaces on paragraphs (no visual effect), and the `^` anchor never matches a space-prefixed line. Going to:

- Indent both `## Todo` intro paragraphs (lines 22-25, 27-32) by 1 leading space per line.
- Indent the `## Bugs` intro paragraph (lines after `## Bugs` heading) by 1.
- Add a sentence to the convention prose making the rule discoverable for future authors.
- Mirror the rule in `fix-todo.py`'s docstring.

Reading the current state of the relevant lines first to be safe.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Renumber `notes/todo.md` entries in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`.

- Matches any leading `N. ` prefix (not only `1. `), so the script
  closes gaps after deletions and absorbs insertions equally well.
- Shifts continuation-line indent by the delta in prefix width
  (e.g. 4→3 when a 2-digit entry becomes 1-digit, or +1 the other
  way). Empty lines pass through.
- Idempotent on already-renumbered content.

Slated for replacement by a Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`
subcommand pair in a future cycle (see notes/todo.md).
"""

import re

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

# Regex for a leading `N. ` on a line — `\d+` is one or more digits,
# `\.` is a literal dot, `\s` is a whitespace character.
NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    # Split the file into lines, walk them, build the new file line-by-line
    # in `out`, then rejoin at the end.
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []

    # State carried across the loop:
    section: str | None = None  # current `## Heading`, or None outside any
    entry_num = 0  # running entry count within `section`
    shift = 0  # for the most-recent entry's continuation
    # lines: how many spaces to add (positive)
    # or remove (negative) from their indent so
    # they stay aligned under the new prefix.

    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith("## "):
            # Section heading — entering a new section; reset counters.

            # Strip trailing spaces
            section = line.strip()

            # Number start with 0 pre-incremented below
            entry_num = 0

            # Actual value calcuated below  on a match
            # If it doesn't match no shifting will be performed
            shift = 0

            # Output section line as is even trailing spaces(?)
            # I wonder if this should be "section" instead?
            out.append(line)
            continue

        # Only the two numbered sections need renumbering.
        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                # Entry first line: starts with `N. `. Renumber to the next
                # sequential value and record the prefix-width delta for the
                # entry's continuation lines that follow.
                old_prefix = m.group(0)  # e.g. "9. "  or "10. "
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "  # e.g. "10. " or "9. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix) :])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue

            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                # Assumes no empty lines between numbered items(?)

                # Continuation line under the current entry. If the entry's
                # prefix changed width (shift != 0), pad or trim the leading
                # spaces by the same amount so the text stays aligned under
                # the new prefix. Blank lines pass through (no indent to fix).
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        # Everything else (blank lines, lines outside the two numbered
        # sections, continuation lines when shift == 0) passes through
        # unchanged.
        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)


def main() -> None:
    with open(PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = renumber(content)
    with open(PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Renumbered todo.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

message.content: We need to document the why and where indentation is needed and should it be 1, 2 or 3 spaces?

content.text:
Two pieces, addressing each:

**How much indent?** **1 space.**

Rationale:
- The convention's job is to dodge the regex (`^\d+\.\s`), not to add visual emphasis. Minimum is the principled answer when more provides no functional benefit.
- 1 space is visually distinct from anything else in the file:
  - 0 spaces → entry first line.
  - 3 spaces → continuation under 1-digit entry, **and** matches today's intro indent — a reader can confuse prose for entry-continuation.
  - 4 spaces → continuation under 2-digit entry.
  - 1 space → unique to intro prose; the oddness itself signals "this means something" (and the convention prose explains what).
- Easier rule to remember: "1 space, no more, no less" beats "≥1 but use 3 to match the example list."

**Where to document?**

Two homes, each load-bearing:

- **`fix-todo.py` docstring** — for someone reading the script, explains why the script's regex assumes column-0 only. Already there; just trim "at least one" to "exactly one" and tighten the WHY.
- **`## Todo` convention prose** — for someone editing the file, explains the rule and points at the script as the reason. Future authors who add a new intro paragraph need to know.

Concretely, three edits:

1. Re-indent `## Todo` intro paragraphs (currently 3 spaces) → 1 space.
2. Re-indent `## Bugs` intro paragraph (currently 3 spaces from my last edit) → 1 space.
3. Add a sentence to the `## Todo` convention prose: "Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` indent **1 space** at the start of every line so they don't match the renumber script's entry regex (`^\d+\.\s`); see `notes/fix-todo.py`."
4. Tighten `fix-todo.py` docstring to "exactly one leading space" (and explain why minimum is right).

Proceed?

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
r"""Renumber `notes/todo.md` entries in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`.

- Matches any leading `N. ` prefix (not only `1. `), so the script
  closes gaps after deletions and absorbs insertions equally well.
- Shifts continuation-line indent by the delta in prefix width
  (e.g. 4→3 when a 2-digit entry becomes 1-digit, or +1 the other
  way). Empty lines pass through.
- Idempotent on already-renumbered content.
- Convention: intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` must
  begin every line with at least one leading space so they don't
  accidentally match the entry regex (`^\d+\.\s`). CommonMark
  strips up to 3 leading spaces on paragraphs, so the indent is
  invisible in the rendered output but breaks the column-0 anchor
  for this script.

Slated for replacement by a Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`
subcommand pair in a future cycle (see notes/todo.md).
"""

import re

PATH = "/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/todo.md"

# Regex for a leading `N. ` on a line — `\d+` is one or more digits,
# `\.` is a literal dot, `\s` is a whitespace character.
NUM_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)\.\s")


def renumber(content: str) -> str:
    """Walk `## Todo` and `## Bugs`, renumber entries, shift continuations."""
    # Split the file into lines, walk them, build the new file line-by-line
    # in `out`, then rejoin at the end.
    lines = content.split("\n")
    out: list[str] = []

    # State carried across the loop:
    section: str | None = None  # current `## Heading`, or None outside any
    entry_num = 0  # running entry count within `section`
    shift = 0  # for the most-recent entry's continuation
    # lines: how many spaces to add (positive)
    # or remove (negative) from their indent so
    # they stay aligned under the new prefix.

    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith("## "):
            # Section heading — entering a new section; reset counters.

            # Strip trailing spaces
            section = line.strip()

            # Number start with 0 pre-incremented below
            entry_num = 0

            # Actual value calcuated below  on a match
            # If it doesn't match no shifting will be performed
            shift = 0

            # Output section line as is even trailing spaces(?)
            # I wonder if this should be "section" instead?
            out.append(line)
            continue

        # Only the two numbered sections need renumbering.
        if section in ("## Todo", "## Bugs"):
            m = NUM_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                # Entry first line: starts with `N. `. Renumber to the next
                # sequential value and record the prefix-width delta for the
                # entry's continuation lines that follow.
                old_prefix = m.group(0)  # e.g. "9. "  or "10. "
                entry_num += 1
                new_prefix = f"{entry_num}. "  # e.g. "10. " or "9. "
                out.append(new_prefix + line[len(old_prefix) :])
                shift = len(new_prefix) - len(old_prefix)
                continue

            if shift != 0 and line.strip() != "":
                # Assumes no empty lines between numbered items(?)

                # Continuation line under the current entry. If the entry's
                # prefix changed width (shift != 0), pad or trim the leading
                # spaces by the same amount so the text stays aligned under
                # the new prefix. Blank lines pass through (no indent to fix).
                leading = len(line) - len(line.lstrip(" "))
                new_leading = max(0, leading + shift)
                out.append(" " * new_leading + line.lstrip(" "))
                continue

        # Everything else (blank lines, lines outside the two numbered
        # sections, continuation lines when shift == 0) passes through
        # unchanged.
        out.append(line)

    return "\n".join(out)


def main() -> None:
    with open(PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
        content = f.read()
    content = renumber(content)
    with open(PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Renumbered todo.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.content:
1	//! The `sync` subcommand: fetch + classify + (optionally) rebase /
2	//! fast-forward a workspace's repos against their remotes.
3	//!
4	//! Default is `--check` (fetch + report, error if any repo needs
5	//! action); `--no-check` applies. On any failure every repo is
6	//! reverted to its pre-sync op via `jj op restore`.
7	//!
8	//! `current_op_id` / `op_restore` are `pub(crate)` so `push` reuses
9	//! the same snapshot-and-restore pattern.
10	
11	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
12	use std::process::Command;
13	
14	use clap::Args;
15	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
16	
17	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, run, scope_to_repos};
18	use crate::context::Context;
19	use crate::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
20	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
21	
22	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
23	///
24	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
25	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
26	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
27	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
28	///
29	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
30	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
31	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
32	///
33	/// Repo set is resolved from `--scope`:
34	///
35	/// - Explicit `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — keyword forms
36	///   resolve via the project root's `.vc-config.toml`; the path
37	///   form syncs that one repo (single-repo mode).
38	/// - No `--scope` — workspace-default scope:
39	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
40	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`
41	///   - POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → cwd as a single-repo target
42	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
43	pub struct SyncArgs {
44	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
45	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
46	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
47	    pub check: bool,
48	
49	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
50	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
51	    pub no_check: bool,
52	
53	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
54	    #[arg(short, long)]
55	    pub quiet: bool,
56	
57	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
58	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
59	    pub bookmark: String,
60	
61	    /// Remote to sync against
62	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
63	    pub remote: String,
64	
65	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
66	    ///
67	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`:
68	    ///
69	    /// - `code` — sync only the app repo.
70	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
71	    ///   is configured).
72	    /// - `code,bot` — sync both repos.
73	    /// - `<path>` (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`) — single-repo mode; sync
74	    ///   only that repo, ignoring `.vc-config.toml`.
75	    ///
76	    /// Default depends on workspace state: dual workspace →
77	    /// `code,bot`; single-repo workspace → `code`; POR → cwd.
78	    #[arg(
79	        short = 's',
80	        long,

content.text:
Opener edits done: todo.md ladder marks 0.53.0-1 (current), Cargo bumped, task #1 completed and #2 in_progress.

Now I want to confirm the design choices before touching source. The cleanup is bigger than just removing `Scope::Single` — three real decisions:

## Decision 1 — `Scope` type after `Single` is gone

With only one variant left, the enum is half-cleanup. Three shapes:

- **(a) Single-variant enum** `Scope::Roles(Vec<Side>)` — minimal churn (every `match` site keeps its `Scope::Roles(v) => …` arm).
- **(b) Newtype struct** `pub struct Scope(pub Vec<Side>)` — drop the variant, accessors via `scope.0` or methods. Cleaner end state, more touch sites.
- **(c) Drop the type entirely**, use `Vec<Side>` everywhere — most cleanup but loses the "this Vec means roles" naming.

The bot leans **(b)**. The whole reason for the enum was distinguishing Roles from Single; without that distinction, the enum is vestigial. Naming the type via newtype keeps the call-site readability.

## Decision 2 — POR's `default_scope` return value

Today, `default_scope(workspace_root=None, cwd)` returns `Scope::Single(cwd)`. After `Single` goes away, two options:

- **POR returns `Roles([Code])`** — identical runtime behavior because `scope_to_repos(Code, workspace_root=None)` already falls back to `PathBuf::from(".")`. Drop the `cwd` parameter from `default_scope` since it's unused.
- Different shape — invent a new variant.

**Lean (i)** — it's a no-op behavior-wise; the migration is just changing what value is constructed.

## Decision 3 — `sync --scope <path>`

Today `vc-x1 sync` accepts `--scope ./foo` as a single-repo sync target. After path-in-scope dies, options:

- **Drop the path form entirely.** Users who want to sync a non-workspace repo can `cd && vc-x1 sync`. If a real use case appears, add `-R/--repo` to sync as a separate todo.
- **Add `-R/--repo` to sync now** to preserve the capability.

**Lean (i)** — drop. Sync's intent is "sync the workspace I'm in." If it turns out to matter, `-R` can land later (already implicit in the `-R/--scope` symmetry we've established for query commands).

OK to proceed on all three leans (newtype `Scope`, POR returns `Roles([Code])`, drop sync's path form)?

content.text:
Yes, the dual-`scope` confusion is real, and looking at it, the cleanest fix isn't a rename — it's **reordering the substeps**.

Today's ladder:
- 0.53.0-1: collapse `src/scope.rs` to roles-only
- 0.53.0-2: delete `options_flags/scope.rs`, replace with `--por` flag

Swap them:
- 0.53.0-1: delete `options_flags/scope.rs`, replace with `--por` flag
- 0.53.0-2: collapse `src/scope.rs` to roles-only

**Why this beats renaming:**
- After the new 0.53.0-1, there's only one module named `scope` in the codebase (`src/scope.rs`). No ambiguity, no rename ceremony, no chores prose explaining "the `scope` you mean depends on context."
- A rename of either file would be import-churn that gets undone (or partially undone) by 0.53.0-2 anyway, since `options_flags/scope.rs` is going away regardless.
- The substeps are independent — different files, different consumers (init/clone/test_helpers vs CommonArgs/sync/common.rs). Order doesn't matter for correctness.

**Cost of the swap:** trivial — update the ladder lines in `todo.md > ## In Progress` and `chores-11.md`'s opener section to reflect the new order. The Cargo.toml bump to `0.53.0-1` stays (only the work assigned to that version changes).

OK to swap? If yes I'll update those two notes files and start on the options_flags work.

message.content: That's reasonable but is /src/scope.rs "is-a" option so it should be in options_flags, I think?
That's reasonable but it looks to me /src/scope.rs is an option, so it should be in options_flags, I think?

content.text:
Right — that's a real structural insight. Once `Scope` is roles-only, it IS just "the typed value and parser for the `-s/--scope` flag," which is exactly what `options_flags/` is for. Co-locating with `common_args.rs` (the inline-fields bundle that consumes it) makes the file's purpose self-evident.

Revised ladder, two changes:

**(a) Swap order** — `options_flags::scope` deletion lands first, so during `0.53.0-2` there's only one `scope` module to think about.

**(b) Fold the move into the collapse step** — the new `0.53.0-2` doesn't just slim `Scope`, it relocates it to its proper home. Two changes share the same review (both touch `src/scope.rs`'s contents + every importer); splitting them into two commits would just be churn.

Final ladder:

| Substep | Work |
|---|---|
| 0.53.0-1 | Delete `options_flags/scope.rs`, replace with `--por` flag leaf. Migrate init, clone, test_helpers. |
| 0.53.0-2 | Collapse `Scope` to roles-only **and** relocate it into `options_flags/scope.rs`. Drop `src/scope.rs`; update all imports `crate::scope::*` → `crate::options_flags::scope::*`. |
| 0.53.0-3 | Docs/script sweep (README, ARCHITECTURE, substep-test.sh, notes refs). |
| 0.53.0 | Close-out. |

After `0.53.0-2`, the answer to "where does `Scope` live?" is "with every other shared CLI value type — in `options_flags/`." The whole rationale for separating runtime `Scope` from CLI-layer `ScopeKind` evaporates because there's only one kind of `Scope` left.

Need to update `todo.md > ## In Progress` and `chores-11.md`'s opener-section ladder to reflect this revision. The `chores-11.md` opener becomes the worked example of the "Significantly modify the shape — recorded in a new chores subsection at the next step" pattern from `0.52.0`'s convention.

OK to proceed with the ladder revision + start the new `0.53.0-1` (options_flags::scope deletion)?

aiTitle: Review options_flags and scope argument design

message.content: ok, explain "roles-only" and also is --scope going to still accept "<path>" | "code" | "bot" or | "code,bot" | "bot,code"?
ok, explain "roles-only" and also is --scope going to still accept "<path>" | "code" | "bot" | "code,bot" | "bot,code"?

content.text:
**"Roles-only" — what's left of `Scope`:**

Today the runtime `Scope` is an enum with two variants:
- `Roles(Vec<Side>)` — the role-bearing form (code / bot / both).
- `Single(PathBuf)` — the path-bearing form (operate on this one repo, ignore `.vc-config.toml`).

"Roles-only" = the `Single(PathBuf)` variant goes away. What remains carries role information only — a `Vec<Side>` wrapped in a newtype (`pub struct Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`).

The variant could disappear because paths are no longer expressible in `--scope` — they live on a separate flag (`-R/--repo`). So the runtime type no longer needs to model "is this a role-set or a path?"

**`--scope` value grammar after the cleanup — your second question:**

| Form | Accepted before? | Accepted after? |
|---|---|---|
| `code` | ✓ | ✓ |
| `bot` | ✓ | ✓ |
| `code,bot` | ✓ | ✓ |
| `bot,code` | ✓ | ✓ |
| `<path>` (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`) | ✓ (today on `sync --scope`; `parse_scope_roles` rejects elsewhere) | **✗ dropped** |
| `por` | ✓ (on `init`/`clone` via `ScopeKind`) | **✗ dropped — replaced by `--por` boolean** |

So `--scope` accepts the four role-keyword forms only after `0.53.0`. No path, no topology. Path-only operation routes through `-R/--repo` (already on the query commands; not on sync today — `cd && vc-x1 sync` is the workaround). Topology choice routes through `--por`.

This matches the three-orthogonal-concerns split documented in `chores-11.md`'s `0.53.0-0` opener:
- **Path** → `-R/--repo`
- **Roles** → `-s/--scope` (the four keywords)
- **Topology** → `--por` boolean

Does that match your understanding, or is something off?

message.content: Maybe sync should accept -R,--repo so you don't have to change directory to sync?

content.text:
Agreed — that's a real UX regression to leave open. Adding `-R/--repo` to sync is small once `Scope` is settled, and it preserves today's "sync a non-workspace repo without cd" capability.

**Where it fits:** bundle into `0.53.0-2` (the Scope collapse + relocate substep). That substep already touches `sync.rs` to consume the relocated `Scope`; adding the `-R` field while there avoids splitting "sync edits" across two commits. Same semantics as CommonArgs's `-R`:

- `-R PATH` alone → sync the single repo at PATH.
- `-R PATH + -s ROLES` → use PATH as workspace root, resolve sides.
- `-s ROLES` alone → resolve against `find_workspace_root()` from cwd.
- Neither → cwd's workspace defaults.

Sync's repo resolution becomes a thin wrapper around the same compose logic CommonArgs uses (or `common::resolve_repos` outright if the defaults line up — minor refactor at most).

**Revised ladder:**

| Substep | Work |
|---|---|
| 0.53.0-1 | Delete `options_flags/scope.rs`; replace with `--por` boolean. Migrate init, clone, test_helpers. |
| 0.53.0-2 | Collapse `Scope` to roles-only; relocate to `options_flags/scope.rs`; **add `-R/--repo` to sync**. |
| 0.53.0-3 | Docs/script sweep. |
| 0.53.0 | Close-out. |

Cycle title at close-out probably becomes `refactor: clean up scope + todo list (0.53.0)` (unchanged from current opener). The sync `-R` addition is small enough to ride along as one bullet in `0.53.0-2`'s chores section, not a new design subsection.

OK to land this revised ladder and start `0.53.0-1`?

content.text:
That's a reasonable split — the cycle has effectively grown into two distinct concerns (notes-side hygiene that's already landed in -0 vs scope CLI cleanup that's all ahead), and capping `0.53.0` here keeps both cycles reviewable.

**Lean: yes, do the split.** The tradeoff is one extra commit (close-out for 0.53.0 + opener for 0.54.0) in exchange for much clearer cycle narratives — each cycle's name accurately describes what landed. The `0.53.0-0` chores opener section stays as-is (it records what was committed); a brief `0.53.0` close-out section notes the re-scope decision and that the captured scope-cleanup design carries forward via reference to `0.54.0-0`. The scope-cleanup design itself isn't lost — it's already documented in chores-11.

Two small wrinkles either way:
- The todo edits already done in 0.53.0-0 (deleted #5/#6/#14, rewrites, dropped `scope` from #2) reflect *design decisions* the user has made, not the scope-cleanup implementation. They stay deleted — `0.54.0` just lands the code that those decisions imply.
- The Cargo bump to `0.53.0-1` from a few turns ago becomes `0.53.0` (close-out), not reverted.

Your call on whether to split now or keep going.

message.content: Change 0.53.0-0 to 0.53.0, except the "plans" related to the ongoing work moves to "0.54.0-0", is that too much work?

content.text:
The mechanical work is small, but it requires **force-push to main on both repos** — `jj describe --ignore-immutable` to rewrite the committed `0.53.0-0` title and body, then `jj git push --bookmark main` which is effectively a force-push since the commit SHA changes.

CLAUDE.md flags force-push to main as something to warn about; it's allowed in "late changes after push" workflows, but each invocation is a thing-to-be-aware-of.

**Two paths to the same end state:**

| Path | Commits | Force-push? | History |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Rewrite** | rewrite -0 → 0.53.0; new commit for 0.54.0-0 | yes, on both repos | clean: 0.53.0 directly records the notes hygiene |
| **Forward-only** | new close-out commit for 0.53.0; new commit for 0.54.0-0 | no | 0.53.0-0 (planned-scope-cleanup title) + 0.53.0 (re-scope close-out section) — honest about the mid-cycle pivot |

The bot leans **forward-only**. Cost is one extra commit (tiny close-out: Cargo bump 0.53.0-1 → 0.53.0, brief chores close-out section noting the re-scope, todo entry to Done, In Progress cleared); benefit is no force-push and a transparent record of the decision. The 0.53.0-0 commit title staying as-is is a minor "this isn't quite what landed" wart — readable in context with the close-out section.

But it's your repo and you'd see the history more than I will. If you want the cleaner shape, the rewrite is well within "doable today." Which way?

message.content: There are no public users, I say rewrite and forch-push
There are no public users, I say rewrite and forch-push, but on the bot repo we should only force push the commit description/title don't change any file content, is that doable?
There are no public users, I say rewrite and forch-push, but on the bot repo we should only force push the commit description/title don't change any file content, is that doable?

content.text:
Doable. Proposed shape:

**Title** (both repos, 43 chars): `chore: todo renumber + fix-todo.py (0.53.0)`

**Body** (both repos, ochid trailer differs):

```
Notes-side hygiene cycle. Originally opened as the broader
scope CLI cleanup; re-scoped at close-out — the notes prep
(renumber convention + fix-todo.py + todo edits reflecting
design decisions) is a separable concern that landed
cleanly; the scope CLI work itself opens fresh as 0.54.0
with a dedicated plan. The captured design (three orthogonal
concerns: path on -R, roles on -s, topology on --por) carries
forward to 0.54.0-0's chores section.

- Cargo.toml: 0.52.0 → 0.53.0.
- notes/fix-todo.py: new — interim Python script that
  renumbers `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entries in todo.md and
  shifts continuation-line indent across prefix-width
  changes; idempotent. Slated for Rust replacement as
  todo #1.
- notes/todo.md: manual `N.` numbering (44 entries after 1
  insert at #1 + 3 deletes + 4 rewrites reflecting design
  decisions for 0.54.0); intro prose indents 1 space per
  line in `## Todo` / `## Bugs` to dodge the renumber regex;
  rule documented at file-top intro for prominence.
- notes/chores/chores-11.md: backfilled `Commits: [[6]]` on
  the 0.52.0 close-out section; new 0.53.0 close-out section
  with as-built notes and re-scope outcome; `[6]:` ref to
  9ba2b672a29f.

ochid: /.claude/sqvkvqmnslym
```

(`.claude` trailer is `ochid: /svtzssytmuox`.)

**Operations:**

1. Edit @ (working copy) to close-out state: Cargo `0.53.0-1` → `0.53.0`; todo `## In Progress` cleared back to placeholder, Done entry added for 0.53.0; chores-11 section rewritten (rename + drop scope-cleanup design content + add re-scope outcome).
2. `jj squash --ignore-immutable -R .` — fold @ into @-.
3. `jj describe @- --ignore-immutable -R . -m "$TITLE" -m "$BODY"` — rewrite app description.
4. `jj describe @- --ignore-immutable -R .claude -m "$TITLE" -m "$BODY"` — rewrite .claude description, file content untouched.
5. `jj git push --bookmark main -R .` — force-push app (commit SHA changed).
6. `jj git push --bookmark main -R .claude` — force-push .claude.
7. `rm -f .vc-x1/push-state.toml` — clear stale push state (defensive; should already be cleared).

content.subject: 0.53.0-1: collapse src/scope.rs to roles-only
content.description: Drop `Scope::Single(PathBuf)`, drop `parse_scope_roles` (folds into `parse_scope`), simplify the grammar to `code|bot|code,bot|bot,code` only. Update `CommonArgs`, `sync`, `common.rs`, `init` runtime call sites. Tests get the same trim.

content.description: Delete `options_flags/scope.rs` (ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind, ScopeParser, ScopeOption). Add a `PorFlag` leaf in `options_flags/` carrying a single boolean. Migrate `init`, `clone`, `test_helpers`, and `init/params.rs` to consume the boolean. Help text: "Create a single plain repo (no .claude/ companion)."

content.subject: 0.53.0-3: docs/script sweep
content.description: Update README.md, notes/substep-test.sh, ARCHITECTURE.md, and any chores/notes references that mention the old `--scope code,bot|por` form. Also notes/README.md if it documents the convention.

content.subject: 0.53.0: close-out
content.description: Final commit dropping the `-N` suffix. notes/todo.md: move In Progress entry to ## Done with [N] ref; update notes/chores/chores-11.md with close-out section + as-built ladder + outcome notes; backfill 0.53.0-3's Commits ref; ensure chores section headers match commit titles. Pre-commit cargo cycle, then push.

input.old_string:
## refactor: clean up scope + todo list (0.53.0-0)

Multi-step. Two `--scope` flags coexist with different
grammars: query-side `-s code|bot|code,bot` (which sides of
an existing workspace) and creation-side `-s code,bot|por`
(workspace topology). Same spelling, different parsers,
different types — the bot ran into them while planning and
miscounted todo entries in the process. The cleanup factors
the three concerns apart:

- **Path** stays on `-R/--repo`. Always present (defaults to
  cwd). Independent of roles.
- **Roles** stay on `-s/--scope`
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`). Single-purpose: which
  side(s) of an existing dual workspace.
- **Topology** moves to a boolean `--por` flag at creation
  time (init, clone). The `code,bot|por` grammar collapses
  to "do you want a `.claude` companion or not."

After this cycle:

- `Scope::Single(PathBuf)` — gone. `-R <path>` is the
  path-only entry point; the `Scope` runtime type holds
  only roles.
- `parse_scope_roles` — gone. Its job (reject path forms in
  the roles slot) is moot once paths aren't a roles form.
- `options_flags/scope.rs` (`ScopeKind`, `ScopeOption`,
  `parse_scope_kind`, `ScopeParser`) — replaced by a
  `PorFlag` leaf in `options_flags/`.

The deeper symmetric-`.vc-config.toml` refactor (todo #11)
is independent and lands in `0.54.0`; keeping it separate
keeps each cycle reviewable.

### Todo items the cycle absorbs

Deletes:

- #5 (was #4): "Drop `-R/--repo` from `CommonArgs`" —
  premised on folding `<path>` into `-s`, rejected here.
- #6 (was #5): "`-s <path>` workspace-root override" —
  same premise, same fate.
- #14 (was #13): "init dual|por arg split" — settled
  with `--por` flag (a third shape neither half of the
  entry considered).

Rewrites / light touch:

- #2 (was #1): drop `scope` from the single-field
  leaves sweep — `ScopeOption` is being deleted.
- #13 (was #12): `--scope` sweep continuation —
  `Single(_)` dogfood half drops out; only the
  `finalize --scope` replacing `--repo` half survives.
- #19 (was #18), #20 (was #19): push/clone
  `--scope=...|<path>` flag designs — `<path>` form
  drops out.
- #21 (was #20): validate-desc/fix-desc `--scope` —
  `Single(_)`-errors-here parenthetical moot.

A new entry #1 (`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo`)
captures the future Rust replacement for the interim
`notes/fix-todo.py`.

### Notes-side prep done at -0

The cycle's todo edits needed clean ordinal references, so
the opener also lands a notes-convention change:

- `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entries now use manual numbering
  (`1.` `2.` …) rather than lazy `1.` everywhere. The raw
  source is readable without rendering, at the cost of
  diff churn on reorder. See the convention prose in
  todo.md.
- `notes/fix-todo.py` — interim Python script that
  renumbers and shifts continuation indent on a delete /
  reorder. Slated for Rust replacement (todo #1).

### Ladder

- 0.53.0-0 plan + version bump + notes convention +
  fix-todo script + todo edits (current)
- 0.53.0-1 collapse `src/scope.rs` to roles-only —
  drop `Scope::Single`, `parse_scope_roles`; simplify
  parser + helpers
- 0.53.0-2 replace `ScopeKind`/`ScopeOption` with
  `--por` flag — delete `options_flags/scope.rs`;
  migrate init / clone / test_helpers
- 0.53.0-3 docs/script sweep — README, ARCHITECTURE,
  substep-test.sh, notes references to the old
  `--scope code,bot|por` form; delete `notes/fix-todo.py`
- 0.53.0 close-out
input.new_string:
## chore: todo renumber + fix-todo.py (0.53.0)

Notes-side hygiene cycle. Originally opened (and committed
under the working title `refactor: clean up scope + todo
list`) as a multi-step scope CLI cleanup, but re-scoped at
the close-out boundary when the notes-side prep turned out
to be a separable concern that landed cleanly on its own.
The scope CLI cleanup opens fresh as `0.54.0` with a
dedicated plan; the captured design (three orthogonal
concerns: path on `-R`, roles on `-s`, topology on `--por`;
`Scope` relocates into `options_flags/scope.rs`) carries
forward to `0.54.0-0`'s chores section.

### What landed

- **Manual `N.` entry numbering** in `## Todo` and `## Bugs`
  (was lazy `1.` everywhere). Each insert / delete / reorder
  bumps numbers in lockstep; `notes/fix-todo.py` automates
  this and shifts continuation-line indent across
  prefix-width changes (3 ↔ 4 spaces).
- **Intro prose indented 1 space** per line in `## Todo` and
  `## Bugs` so it doesn't accidentally match the renumber
  regex (`^\d+\.\s`). Convention documented at the top of
  `todo.md` (and mirrored in `notes/fix-todo.py`'s
  docstring).
- **44 entries** after 1 insert (#1 — future `vc-x1
  fix-todo` Rust subcommand), 3 deletes (was #4/#5/#13 —
  design decisions superseded their content), 4 rewrites
  (was #12/#18/#19/#20 — `Single(_)` and `<path>` references
  dropped to reflect 0.54.0's design).

### Outcome — re-scope note

The cycle was opened bundling notes-side prep with the scope
CLI cleanup. Mid-cycle, several observations pulled the two
apart:

- The notes-side change (renumbering, fix-todo.py, intro
  indent rule) was substantial enough on its own to warrant
  a clean review.
- The scope CLI cleanup grew during planning — sync gained
  `-R/--repo`, the runtime `Scope` type wanted relocation
  into `options_flags/`, the substep ladder re-ordered.
  Each tweak compounded.
- Keeping both in one cycle would muddy both narratives.

Closing `0.53.0` here as notes hygiene only keeps the two
cycles' reviews independent.

Renumber todo.md (done), backfill chores-11 Commits ref for 0.52.0, delete todos #4/#5/#13, rewrite #12/#18/#19/#20 light touch, drop `scope` from #1's target list, add In Progress ladder, bump Cargo.toml 0.52.0 → 0.53.0-0, add chores-11 section "refactor: clean up scope + todo list (0.53.0-0)", run pre-commit cargo cycle.

content.subject: 0.53.0-1: collapse src/scope.rs to roles-only
content.description: Drop `Scope::Single(PathBuf)`, drop `parse_scope_roles` (folds into `parse_scope`), simplify the grammar to `code|bot|code,bot|bot,code` only. Update `CommonArgs`, `sync`, `common.rs`, `init` runtime call sites. Tests get the same trim.

content.subject: 0.53.0-2: replace ScopeKind/ScopeOption with --por flag
content.description: Delete `options_flags/scope.rs` (ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind, ScopeParser, ScopeOption). Add a `PorFlag` leaf in `options_flags/` carrying a single boolean. Migrate `init`, `clone`, `test_helpers`, and `init/params.rs` to consume the boolean. Help text: "Create a single plain repo (no .claude/ companion)."

content.subject: 0.53.0-3: docs/script sweep
content.description: Update README.md, notes/substep-test.sh, ARCHITECTURE.md, and any chores/notes references that mention the old `--scope code,bot|por` form. Also notes/README.md if it documents the convention.

content.subject: 0.53.0: close-out
content.description: Final commit dropping the `-N` suffix. notes/todo.md: move In Progress entry to ## Done with [N] ref; update notes/chores/chores-11.md with close-out section + as-built ladder + outcome notes; backfill 0.53.0-3's Commits ref; ensure chores section headers match commit titles. TITLE='chore: todo renumber + fix-todo.py (0.53.0)'

BODY_APP=$(cat <<'EOF'
Notes-side hygiene cycle. Originally opened as the broader
scope CLI cleanup; re-scoped at close-out — the notes prep
(renumber convention + fix-todo.py + todo edits reflecting
design decisions) is a separable concern that landed
cleanly; the scope CLI work itself opens fresh as 0.54.0
with a dedicated plan. The captured design (three orthogonal
concerns: path on -R, roles on -s, topology on --por) carries
forward to 0.54.0-0's chores section.

- Cargo.toml: 0.52.0 → 0.53.0.
- notes/fix-todo.py: new — interim Python script that
  renumbers `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entries in todo.md and
  shifts continuation-line indent across prefix-width
  changes; idempotent. Slated for Rust replacement as
  todo #1.
- notes/todo.md: manual `N.` numbering (44 entries after 1
  insert at #1 + 3 deletes + 4 rewrites reflecting design
  decisions for 0.54.0); intro prose indents 1 space per

  rule documented at file-top intro for prominence.

  the 0.52.0 close-out section; new 0.53.0 close-out section
  with as-built notes and re-scope outcome; `[6]:` ref to
  9ba2b672a29f.

ochid: /.claude/sqvkvqmnslym
EOF
)

BODY_CLAUDE=$(cat <<'EOF'

echo "--- squash app ---"
jj squash --ignore-immutable -R . && \
echo "--- describe app ---" && \
jj describe @- --ignore-immutable -R . -m "$TITLE" -m "$BODY_APP" && \
echo "--- describe claude ---" && \
jj describe @- --ignore-immutable -R .claude -m "$TITLE" -m "$BODY_CLAUDE" && \
echo "--- push app ---" && \
jj git push --bookmark main -R . && \
echo "--- push claude ---" && \
jj git push --bookmark main -R .claude && \
echo "--- clear push state ---" && \
rm -f .vc-x1/push-state.toml && \
echo "DONE"

input.old_string:
Closing `0.53.0` here as notes hygiene only keeps the two
cycles' reviews independent.

# References
input.new_string:
Closing `0.53.0` here as notes hygiene only keeps the two
cycles' reviews independent.

## refactor: scope CLI cleanup (0.54.0-0)

Multi-step. Today, two `--scope` flags coexist with
different value grammars: query-side `-s code|bot|code,bot`
(which sides of an existing workspace) and creation-side
`-s code,bot|por` (workspace topology). The cleanup factors
the three concerns apart onto three orthogonal flags:

- **Path** — `-R/--repo PATH`. Always present (defaults to
  cwd). Independent of role selection.
- **Roles** — `-s/--scope code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`.
  Single-purpose: which side(s) of an existing dual
  workspace.
- **Topology** — `--por` boolean. Creation-only (init,
  clone). The `code,bot|por` grammar collapses to "do
  you want a `.claude` companion or not."

After this cycle:

- `Scope::Single(PathBuf)` — gone. `-R PATH` is the
  path-only entry point.
- `parse_scope_roles` — gone. Its job (reject path forms
  in the roles slot) is moot once paths aren't a roles
  form.
- `options_flags/scope.rs` (`ScopeKind`, `ScopeOption`,
  `parse_scope_kind`, `ScopeParser`) — replaced by a
  `PorFlag` boolean leaf in `options_flags/`.
- `src/scope.rs` — relocated into
  `options_flags/scope.rs` (no longer ambiguous with the
  deleted file). `Scope` becomes a newtype
  `pub struct Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`; the `Single` variant
  goes away.
- `vc-x1 sync` gains `-R/--repo` for single-repo or
  workspace-root override, parallel to `CommonArgs`'s
  `-R`. Preserves today's `sync --scope <path>`
  capability after path-in-scope drops.

The design was captured during `0.53.0`'s opener (committed
under the working title `refactor: clean up scope + todo
list` then re-scoped) and carries forward here. The deeper
symmetric-`.vc-config.toml` refactor (todo #11) is
independent and lands in a later cycle.

### Ladder

- 0.54.0-0 plan + version bump + chores opener (current)
- 0.54.0-1 delete `options_flags/scope.rs`; replace with
  `--por` boolean flag leaf; migrate init / clone /
  test_helpers
- 0.54.0-2 collapse `Scope` to roles-only (newtype over
  `Vec<Side>`); relocate `src/scope.rs` into
  `options_flags/scope.rs`; drop `parse_scope_roles`;
  add `-R/--repo` to sync
- 0.54.0-3 docs/script sweep — README, ARCHITECTURE,
  substep-test.sh, notes references to the old
  `--scope code,bot|por` form; delete
  `notes/fix-todo.py`
- 0.54.0 close-out

### Per-step evaluation

Effectiveness is evaluated after each substep. Possible
outcomes at any step boundary:

- Continue as planned.
- Significantly modify the shape — recorded in a new
  chores subsection at the next step.
- Abandon: revert the cycle to a no-op with an
  `### Outcome` note capturing what didn't fit.

# References

content.text:
`0.54.0-0` opener edits in place.

**Changes:**
- `notes/chores/chores-11.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[7]]` on the 0.53.0 close-out section; new `## refactor: scope CLI cleanup (0.54.0-0)` opener with the three-orthogonal-concerns design (`-R` / `-s` / `--por`), after-this-cycle subtractions, and the four-substep ladder; new `[7]:` ref to `6a4a193b8f0d`.
- `Cargo.toml`: `0.53.0` → `0.54.0-0`.
- `notes/todo.md`: `## In Progress` ladder populated with the 0.54.0 substeps; `0.54.0-0` marked `(current)`.
- Tasks #6–#10 created; `#6` in_progress.

Ready for review. On approval I'll run the pre-commit cycle and prep the commit title/body for the second approval.

content.content:
1	//! `vc-x1 clone` — clone a repo (URL or local path) into a workspace.
2	//!
3	//! - `--scope=code,bot` (default): dual-repo layout — clones code,
4	//!   derives bot source (`<source>.claude`), clones bot into
5	//!   `<target>/.claude`, creates the Claude Code symlink. Both
6	//!   sides must succeed.
7	//! - `--scope=por`: single repo into `<target>`. No `.claude/`,
8	//!   no symlink.
9	//!
10	//! TARGET shapes (all routed through `parse_target`): URL,
11	//! `owner/name` shorthand, or a local path (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`,
12	//! `.`, `..`). Path-form is symmetric with `git clone /local/bare.git`
13	//! — useful for fixtures and CI scratch dirs.
14	//!
15	//! `clone_one` and `clone_dual` are `pub(crate)` so init's `-3`
16	//! reshape can reuse them for the "URL exists → clone" preflight
17	//! path (per `notes/chores/chores-08.md > init + clone redesign`).
18	
19	use std::path::Path;
20	
21	use clap::Args;
22	use log::info;
23	
24	use crate::common::run;
25	use crate::context::Context;
26	use crate::options_flags::scope::{ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind};
27	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
28	use crate::symlink;
29	use crate::url::{Target, derive_name, derive_session_url, parse_target, resolve_url};
30	
31	/// CLI args for `vc-x1 clone`.
32	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
33	pub struct CloneArgs {
34	    /// Source to clone — URL, owner/name shorthand, or local path.
35	    ///
36	    /// - URL: `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
37	    /// - owner/name shorthand: resolves to
38	    ///   `git@github.com:owner/name.git`
39	    /// - Local path: `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`, `.`, `..`
40	    ///   (passed directly to `git clone`)
41	    #[arg(value_name = "TARGET", verbatim_doc_comment)]
42	    pub target: String,
43	
44	    /// Destination dir name in cwd [default: derived from TARGET]
45	    #[arg(value_name = "NAME")]
46	    pub name: Option<String>,
47	
48	    /// What to clone: `code,bot` (dual, default) or `por` (single).
49	    #[arg(
50	        long,
51	        short,
52	        value_name = "SCOPE",
53	        value_parser = parse_scope_kind,
54	        default_value = "code,bot"
55	    )]
56	    pub scope: ScopeKind,
57	
58	    /// Dry run — show what would be done without executing.
59	    #[arg(long)]
60	    pub dry_run: bool,
61	}
62	
63	/// Inputs to the clone op, flat, owned, clap-free.
64	///
65	/// - `target`: the `TARGET` positional (URL, `owner/name`, or
66	///   local path).
67	/// - `name`: optional `NAME` positional override for the
68	///   destination dir.
69	/// - `scope`: `--scope` resolved (default `code,bot`).
70	/// - `dry_run`: `--dry-run`.
71	pub struct CloneParams {
72	    pub target: String,
73	    pub name: Option<String>,
74	    pub scope: ScopeKind,
75	    pub dry_run: bool,
76	}
77	
78	impl From<&CloneArgs> for CloneParams {

content.text:
Clone tests pass (7 tests). Unit B complete.

**Unit B summary** (clone.rs):
- Module docs: replaced `--scope=code,bot` / `--scope=por` with default vs `--por` framing.
- Imports: `ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind` → `PorFlag`.
- `CloneArgs`: `pub scope: ScopeKind` → `pub por: PorFlag` (flattened leaf).
- `CloneParams`: `pub scope: ScopeKind` → `pub por: bool`.
- `From<&CloneArgs>`: `scope: a.scope` → `por: a.por.value`.
- Body: `match params.scope { Por / CodeBot }` → `if params.por { … } else { … }` (both the dry-run dispatch and the real dispatch).
- `clone_dual` doc: dropped the `--scope=code,bot` / `--scope=por` references.
- Tests: pruned 5 obsolete `scope_*` tests; updated `defaults` and `all_opts`; added a `por_flag` smoke test.

Bonus: refined `PorFlag` to use the `value`-field convention from options_flags/README (`#[arg(long = "por")] pub value: bool`) so consumers read `args.por.value` rather than `args.por.por`.

Stopping for review. Next is Unit C — migrate `init/params.rs` + `init.rs`.

message.content: go

content.content:
1	//! Flat plain-struct input to the init op.
2	//!
3	//! `InitParams` is the clap-free shape that `pub fn init` reads
4	//! from. Built `From<&InitArgs>` at the binary edge (main.rs);
5	//! callers without clap (tests, future TUI / library embedding)
6	//! construct it directly.
7	//!
8	//! Per `notes/chores/chores-09.md > ## InitParams implementation
9	//! (0.44.0)`: domain types pass through (`RepoSelector`,
10	//! `ScopeKind`, `ConfigKind`, `PushRetryOptions`); leaf clap
11	//! wrappers do not.
12	
13	use crate::config::RepoSelector;
14	use crate::init::InitArgs;
15	use crate::options_flags::config::ConfigKind;
16	use crate::options_flags::push_retry::PushRetryOptions;
17	use crate::options_flags::scope::ScopeKind;
18	
19	/// Inputs to the init op, flat, owned, clap-free.
20	///
21	/// - `target`: the TARGET positional argument (URL, owner/name,
22	///   path, or bare NAME).
23	/// - `name`: optional `NAME` positional override.
24	/// - `account`: `--account` value (None ⇒ default account chain).
25	/// - `repo`: `--repo` value parsed into a `RepoSelector`.
26	/// - `scope`: `--scope` resolved (default `code,bot`).
27	/// - `private`: `--private`.
28	/// - `dry_run`: `--dry-run`.
29	/// - `push_retry`: `--push-retries` / `--push-retry-delay`.
30	/// - `use_template`: `--use-template` value.
31	/// - `config`: resolved `--config` value (None ⇒ canned write).
32	/// - `create_symlink`: whether `init` should create the
33	///   `~/.claude/projects/` symlink for dual-scope runs (production
34	///   path = true; test fixtures suppress with false).
35	pub struct InitParams {
36	    pub target: String,
37	    pub name: Option<String>,
38	    pub account: Option<String>,
39	    pub repo: Option<RepoSelector>,
40	    pub scope: ScopeKind,
41	    pub private: bool,
42	    pub dry_run: bool,
43	    pub push_retry: PushRetryOptions,
44	    pub use_template: Option<String>,
45	    pub config: Option<ConfigKind>,
46	    pub create_symlink: bool,
47	}
48	
49	impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams {
50	    /// Convert clap-derived `InitArgs` into the flat `InitParams`.
51	    ///
52	    /// Production path: sets `create_symlink: true`. Tests that
53	    /// need `create_symlink: false` construct `InitParams`
54	    /// directly (or post-edit the field).
55	    fn from(a: &InitArgs) -> Self {
56	        Self {
57	            target: a.target.clone(),
58	            name: a.name.clone(),
59	            account: a.account.account.clone(),
60	            repo: a.repo.repo.clone(),
61	            scope: a.scope.scope,
62	            private: a.provision.private.private,
63	            dry_run: a.provision.dry_run.dry_run,
64	            push_retry: a.provision.push_retry.clone(),
65	            use_template: a.use_template.use_template.clone(),
66	            config: a.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None),
67	            create_symlink: true,
68	        }
69	    }
70	}
71

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags

Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
type, parser, and tests stay together.

This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for the options_flags
extraction in [`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md);
see there for how the leaves fit the wider clap-args /
`Context`+`Params` split.

## Architecture

Three composition patterns:

- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
  consumer.
- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` shared by N subcommands,
  picked up with one `#[command(flatten)]` line. Two forms:
  - *flatten-of-leaves* — composes existing leaves into a named
    role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle` = `dry_run` +
    `private` + `push_retry`). Use this when the constituent
    flags are themselves reused elsewhere.
  - *inline-fields* — holds the `#[arg]` fields directly (e.g.
    `common_args::CommonArgs` — the read-only commit-query arg
    set). Use this when a whole *set* of args is shared but the
    individual flags aren't reused outside the bundle —
    extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse. If one of the
    fields ever *is* reused (a future command wants just that
    flag), extract it into a leaf then and
    `#[command(flatten)]` it into the bundle.
- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
  leaf's typed value and parser.

## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax

Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
domain it carries:

- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
  name suffix: `*Flag`.
- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).

Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.

## Adding a new leaf

1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
   (non-boolean).
3. Name the field(s):
   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
     Caveat: clap derives an arg's *id* from the field name, so
     if two `value`-field leaves can ever be flattened into the
     same struct, give each an explicit `#[arg(id = "<unique>")]`
     (else `clap_builder` panics — duplicate arg id `value`).
   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
     flag from its field name as usual.
   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
   handles them.
6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.

## Consuming an OF

Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    #[command(flatten)]
    pub config: ConfigOption,
    // ...
}
```

Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
          verbatim_doc_comment)]
    pub config: Option<String>,
    // ...
}
```

A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
attributes.

## Consumer function shape

Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
fields at the call site:

```rust
// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }

run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
```

This wins on:
- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
  choice).
- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
  zero-touch change at every call site.

For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.

## Marker traits

Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
(their category lives in the type-name suffix).

- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is an Option).
- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.

Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):

- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.

`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
targets).

All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
the appropriate ones explicitly.

## Bundle marker discipline

Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.

## Pattern A worked example

`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
worked example.

## Layout note

OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
not done preemptively.

## refactor: add --por flag; delete options_flags/scope.rs (0.54.0-1)

First substep. The creation-side topology question — "dual
workspace or single repo?" — was previously a value of
`--scope` (`code,bot|por`) parsed into a `ScopeKind` enum.
It's more naturally a boolean: `--por` or not. The substep
introduces a `PorFlag` boolean leaf in `options_flags/por.rs`
and migrates `init` / `clone` / `test_helpers` to consume it;
the old `options_flags/scope.rs` (`ScopeKind`, `ScopeOption`,
`parse_scope_kind`, `ScopeParser`) is deleted.

- `PorFlag` is the first leaf written to the `value`-field
  convention from
  [options_flags/README.md](README.md#adding-a-new-leaf):
  the inner field is `value`, the flag is declared via
  `#[arg(long = "por")]`, and consumers read `args.por.value`
  rather than the doubled `args.por.por`. Existing
  single-field leaves (`dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
  `config`, `use_template`, `repo`) predate this and are
  tracked for migration in `notes/todo.md > #2`.
- Consumer dispatch collapses from a two-arm `match scope
  { CodeBot / Por }` to a single boolean check. Both the
  dry-run print path and the real dispatch path in `init`
  / `clone` shrink; the error messages follow the same
  rename (`--scope=por` → `--por`,
  `--config is only valid with --scope=por` →
  `--config is only valid with --por`).
- Tests prune the obsolete `--scope=*` / `-s *` parses —
  no value space left for `--scope` to error on. Net unit
  test count drops 10: 5 clone `scope_*` tests + 1 init
  `scope_short_flag_por` + 6 `options_flags::scope_kind_*`
  tests deleted; 2 new `por_flag*` smoke tests added.
  366 unit + 14 integration tests pass.
- `Cargo.toml`: `0.54.0-0` → `0.54.0-1`.
- `notes/chores/chores-11.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[8]]`
  on the 0.54.0-0 opener.
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.54.0-0 → `(done)`; 0.54.0-1 marked
  `(current)` (start of substep), flipped to `(done)`
  here (end of substep).

# References
