aiTitle: Review options_flags and scope argument design

content.text:
Pre-commit cycle clean: 366 unit + 14 integration tests pass, binary at `0.54.0-1`.

**Title** (40 chars):
```
refactor: add --por flag leaf (0.54.0-1)
```

(The chores section header currently says `add --por flag; delete options_flags/scope.rs` — I'll sync it to the shorter commit title as the last edit before push.)

**Body** (wrapped ≤72; per-file):
```
First substep of the scope CLI cleanup. The creation-side
topology question — "dual workspace or single repo?" —
moves from a value-bearing `--scope` (`code,bot|por`)
parsed into a `ScopeKind` enum to a plain boolean flag
(`--por` or not). The old `options_flags/scope.rs`
(`ScopeKind`, `ScopeOption`, `parse_scope_kind`,
`ScopeParser`) is deleted; a new `PorFlag` boolean leaf in
`options_flags/por.rs` takes over. Design narrative in
chores-11.

- src/options_flags/por.rs: new — `PorFlag` boolean leaf,
  first written to the `value`-field convention
  (consumers read `args.por.value`).
- src/options_flags/mod.rs: `pub mod por;` added;
  `pub mod scope;` removed.
- src/options_flags/scope.rs: deleted.
- src/options_flags/README.md: dropped stale `ScopeParser`
  example.
- src/clone.rs: inline `pub scope: ScopeKind` →
  `#[command(flatten)] pub por: PorFlag,`; match dispatch
  → if/else; pruned five `scope_*` tests, added `por_flag`
  smoke test.
- src/init.rs: flattened `pub scope: ScopeOption` →
  `pub por: PorFlag`; rewrote plan_init's scope match,
  the two error messages (`--scope=por` → `--por`,
  `--config is only valid with --scope=por` →
  `--config is only valid with --por`), and the
  create_dual/create_por dispatch.
- src/init/params.rs: `pub scope: ScopeKind` →
  `pub por: bool`; From impl mirrors.
- src/init/tests.rs: all `args.scope.scope = ScopeKind::*`
  → `args.por.value = bool`; renamed
  `config_rejected_with_scope_code_bot` →
  `config_rejected_without_por`; replaced
  `scope_short_flag_por` with `por_flag_parses`; error
  message string checks updated.
- src/test_helpers.rs: dual fixture's `scope: ScopeOption`
  → `por: PorFlag { value: false }`; POR fixture's
  mirror with `value: true`.
- tests/cli_init.rs: `--scope=por` → `--por`; dropped
  the no-longer-meaningful `--scope=code,bot` from the
  dual test (dual is now the default).
- Cargo.toml: 0.54.0-0 → 0.54.0-1.
- notes/chores/chores-11.md: backfilled `Commits: [[8]]`
  on the 0.54.0-0 opener; new 0.54.0-1 section; `[8]:`
  ref to d272de2a15a5.
- notes/todo.md: 0.54.0-0 → `(done)`; 0.54.0-1 set
  `(current)` then flipped `(done)`.
```

OK to sync the chores header and push?

message.content: simplify - no need to mention "flattened" and the "; ..." is to much detail. Just added --por removed --scope or something like that.

message.content: In src/clone.rs and init.rs you say --scope -> --por I think that comment should be --scope=por -> --por, although might be mistaken.

content.text:
The commit's already pushed, so this is about whether to amend it.

On the accuracy: `--scope → --por` was shorthand for "the `--scope` flag is replaced by `--por`" — true at the flag level (clone/init no longer have a `--scope` flag). But you've got a point that it reads ambiguously.

Neither `--scope → --por` nor `--scope=por → --por` is fully precise though:
- `--scope=por → --por` describes only the single-repo case — it omits that `--scope=code,bot` → default (no flag).
- The fully accurate form is `--scope=code,bot|por → --por` — the whole value-bearing flag collapses to a boolean.

The chores-11.md section in the same commit already says it correctly ("`--scope=code,bot|por` becomes a `--por` boolean"); only the two commit-body bullets use the loose shorthand.

Fixing the commit body means `jj describe --ignore-immutable` + force-push on both repos (the commit is pushed). For a two-bullet wording nuance, that's heavy ceremony — but you've said force-push is low-cost here and you care about the record being right.

Options:
1. **Leave it** — the chores section (the durable design record) is accurate; the commit body shorthand is imperfect but not wrong.
2. **Amend** — rewrite both bullets to `--scope=code,bot|por → --por` and force-push.

Which?

lastPrompt: In src/clone.rs and init.rs you say --scope -> --por I think that comment should be --scope=por -> --por, although might be mistaken.

content.content:
42	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
43	pub struct SyncArgs {
44	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
45	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
46	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
47	    pub check: bool,
48	
49	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
50	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
51	    pub no_check: bool,
52	
53	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
54	    #[arg(short, long)]
55	    pub quiet: bool,
56	
57	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
58	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
59	    pub bookmark: String,
60	
61	    /// Remote to sync against
62	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
63	    pub remote: String,
64	
65	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
66	    ///
67	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`:
68	    ///
69	    /// - `code` — sync only the app repo.
70	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
71	    ///   is configured).
72	    /// - `code,bot` — sync both repos.
73	    /// - `<path>` (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`) — single-repo mode; sync
74	    ///   only that repo, ignoring `.vc-config.toml`.
75	    ///
76	    /// Default depends on workspace state: dual workspace →
77	    /// `code,bot`; single-repo workspace → `code`; POR → cwd.
78	    #[arg(
79	        short = 's',
80	        long,
81	        value_name = "SCOPE",
82	        value_parser = parse_scope,
83	        verbatim_doc_comment
84	    )]
85	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
86	}
87	
88	/// Inputs to the sync op, flat, owned, clap-free.
89	///
90	/// - `quiet`: `-q` / `--quiet` — clamp output to `Warn` for the run.
91	/// - `bookmark`: bookmark to sync in each repo (default `main`).
92	/// - `remote`: remote to sync against (default `origin`).
93	/// - `no_check`: `--no-check` — actually rebase / fast-forward
94	///   (absent ⇒ check mode: fetch + report only). The `--check`
95	///   flag is the explicit form of the default and carries no
96	///   value of its own, so it isn't mirrored here.
97	/// - `scope`: `--scope` parsed (None ⇒ resolve via the
98	///   workspace-default scope at run time).
99	pub struct SyncParams {
100	    pub quiet: bool,
101	    pub bookmark: String,
102	    pub remote: String,
103	    pub no_check: bool,
104	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
105	}
106	
107	impl From<&SyncArgs> for SyncParams {
108	    /// Convert clap-derived `SyncArgs` into the flat `SyncParams`.
109	    /// `--check` is dropped — it's the explicit form of the
110	    /// default; the op only ever consults `no_check`.
111	    fn from(a: &SyncArgs) -> Self {
112	        Self {
113	            quiet: a.quiet,
114	            bookmark: a.bookmark.clone(),
115	            remote: a.remote.clone(),
116	            no_check: a.no_check,
117	            scope: a.scope.clone(),
118	        }
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	impl SubcommandRunner for SyncArgs {
123	    type Params = SyncParams;
124	
125	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&SyncArgs>` impl above
126	    /// (total — never fails).
127	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
128	        Ok(SyncParams::from(self))
129	    }
130	
131	    /// Run the existing `sync` op.
132	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
133	        sync(ctx, params)
134	    }
135	}
136	
137	/// Resolve `params` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos`
138	/// operates on.
139	///
140	/// Explicit `--scope` wins; otherwise resolves via `default_scope`
141	/// against the discovered workspace root and cwd.
142	fn resolve_params_to_repos(
143	    params: &SyncParams,
144	) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
145	    let workspace_root = find_workspace_root();
146	    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?;
147	    let scope = match &params.scope {
148	        Some(s) => s.clone(),
149	        None => default_scope(workspace_root.as_deref(), &cwd),
150	    };
151	    scope_to_repos(&scope, workspace_root.as_deref())
152	}
153	
154	/// Relationship between a local bookmark and its remote counterpart.
155	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
156	pub enum State {
157	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
158	    UpToDate,
159	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
160	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
161	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
162	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
163	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
164	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
165	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
166	    NoRemote,
167	}
168

170	#[derive(Debug)]
171	struct RepoCtx {

656	///

lines: +/// `-R PATH` parses into the `repo` field; `--scope` stays None.

content.content:
1	//! Integration tests for the sync module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `sync_repos` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures. Each test builds an isolated fixture (bare-git
5	//! remotes + colocated jj repos) under a unique tempdir via
6	//! `Fixture::new`, then drives the scenario with plain `jj`
7	//! subprocess calls. Requires `jj` in `PATH`.
8	//!
9	//! Fixtures clean themselves up via `Fixture`'s `Drop` impl so a
10	//! panicking test still removes its tempdir.
11	
12	use super::*;
13	use crate::scope::Side;
14	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
15	use std::fs;
16	use std::process::Command;
17	
18	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root_from, scope_to_repos};
19	
20	/// Resolver helpers must consume what `init --repo-local` produces.
21	///
22	/// Builds a real dual-repo fixture (bare git remotes + colocated jj
23	/// repos + the canonical `.vc-config.toml` pair init writes), then
24	/// drives the resolver chain `sync()` uses when `-R` is empty:
25	///
26	/// - `find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude)` walks up to `fx.work`
27	///   (proves cwd-portability against init's `path = "/.claude"` /
28	///   `path = "/"` config split).
29	/// - `default_scope(Some(&fx.work))` reads the workspace config
30	///   and resolves to the dual-repo default.
31	/// - `scope_to_repos` maps each `Scope` shape to the right
32	///   absolute path(s) under the fixture.
33	///
34	/// Pure check on the resolver chain — does not invoke `sync()`
35	/// itself, since that walks `std::env::current_dir()` and parallel
36	/// `cargo test` makes cwd mutation unsafe.
37	#[test]
38	fn resolver_chain_against_init_repo_local() {
39	    let fx = Fixture::new("resolver-chain");
40	
41	    // Walk-up from the bot side lands on the app root.
42	    assert_eq!(
43	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude).as_deref(),
44	        Some(&*fx.work),
45	        "find_workspace_root should resolve from .claude up to work"
46	    );
47	    // Walk-up from the app root finds itself.
48	    assert_eq!(
49	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.work).as_deref(),
50	        Some(&*fx.work)
51	    );
52	
53	    // init --repo-local writes other-repo = ".claude", so the
54	    // workspace's default scope is dual; cwd is irrelevant here.
55	    assert_eq!(
56	        default_scope(Some(&fx.work), Path::new(".")),
57	        Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot])
58	    );
59	
60	    // Each scope shape resolves to the right absolute path(s).
61	    assert_eq!(
62	        scope_to_repos(&Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
63	        vec![fx.work.clone(), fx.claude.clone()]
64	    );
65	    assert_eq!(
66	        scope_to_repos(&Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
67	        vec![fx.work.clone()]
68	    );
69	    assert_eq!(
70	        scope_to_repos(&Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
71	        vec![fx.claude.clone()]
72	    );
73	
74	    // sync_repos accepts the resolved list and reports up-to-date
75	    // — the resolver's output is shaped the way sync expects.
76	    let resolved =
77	        scope_to_repos(&Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap();
78	    sync_repos(&resolved, &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed on resolved repos");
79	}
80	
81	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and assert success; returns trimmed stdout.
82	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
83	    let out = Command::new("jj")
84	        .args(args)
85	        .arg("-R")

## refactor: relocate Scope, add sync -R (0.54.0-2)

Second substep. `src/scope.rs` moves into
`options_flags/scope.rs`. Once `ScopeKind` was deleted in
-1, the runtime `Scope` type is just "the typed value +
parser for the `-s/--scope` flag" — exactly what
`options_flags/` houses. The move also resolves the
dual-`scope` naming ambiguity: there is now one `scope`
module, period.

Alongside the relocation:

- `Scope` collapses from a two-variant enum
  (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` | `Single(PathBuf)`) to a newtype
  `Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`. The `Single` variant — the
  path-bearing single-repo escape hatch — is gone; paths
  live on `-R/--repo`. `parse_scope` now accepts only the
  four role keywords; `parse_scope_roles` (the wrapper
  that rejected path forms) is deleted, as is the unused
  `Display` impl.
- `default_scope` drops its `cwd` parameter. Its POR
  branch returned `Single(cwd)`; it now returns
  `Scope([Code])`, which `scope_to_repos` resolves to
  cwd's `.` when no workspace root is given — identical
  runtime behavior, no path variant needed.
- `vc-x1 sync` gains `-R/--repo`, parallel to
  `CommonArgs`'s `-R`. Today's `sync --scope <path>`
  single-repo capability (lost when path-in-scope was
  dropped) is preserved as `sync -R <path>`.
  `resolve_params_to_repos` becomes the four-case
  `(repo, scope)` compose, matching
  `common::resolve_repos` except the no-flags case keeps
  sync's workspace-default behavior.

# References

content.subject: 0.54.0-0: plan + version bump + chores opener
content.description: Cycle opener for scope CLI cleanup. Backfill 0.53.0 Commits ref, bump Cargo 0.53.0 → 0.54.0-0, new chores section with three-orthogonal-concerns design + substep ladder, todo In Progress ladder populated, pre-commit cycle, push.

content.subject: 0.54.0-1: delete options_flags/scope.rs + --por flag
content.description: Delete `options_flags/scope.rs` (ScopeKind, ScopeOption, parse_scope_kind, ScopeParser). Add a PorFlag boolean leaf in `options_flags/`. Migrate init, clone, test_helpers from ScopeKind/ScopeOption to the new --por flag. Help text + tests.

content.subject: 0.54.0-2: collapse Scope + relocate + sync -R
content.description: Drop Scope::Single(PathBuf); make Scope a newtype `pub struct Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`. Drop parse_scope_roles. Relocate src/scope.rs into options_flags/scope.rs and update all imports `crate::scope::*` → `crate::options_flags::scope::*`. POR default_scope returns Roles([Code]). Add `-R/--repo` to sync. Update CommonArgs help text. Tests.

content.subject: 0.54.0-3: docs/script sweep
content.description: Update README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, notes/substep-test.sh, and any other docs/notes that reference the old `--scope code,bot|por` form or path-in-scope. Delete notes/fix-todo.py (replaced by manual renumber going forward until the Rust subcommand lands per todo #1).

content.subject: 0.54.0: close-out
content.description: Final commit dropping the -N suffix. notes/todo.md: In Progress entry → ## Done; chores-11.md close-out section with as-built ladder + outcome; backfill 0.54.0-3's Commits; ensure chores section headers match commit titles. Pre-commit cycle, push.

aiTitle: Review options_flags and scope argument design

attachment.snippet:
1	//! Integration tests for the sync module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `sync_repos` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures. Each test builds an isolated fixture (bare-git
5	//! remotes + colocated jj repos) under a unique tempdir via
6	//! `Fixture::new`, then drives the scenario with plain `jj`
7	//! subprocess calls. Requires `jj` in `PATH`.
8	//!
9	//! Fixtures clean themselves up via `Fixture`'s `Drop` impl so a
10	//! panicking test still removes its tempdir.
11	
12	use super::*;
13	use crate::options_flags::scope::Side;
14	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
15	use std::fs;
16	use std::process::Command;
17	
18	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root_from, scope_to_repos};
19	
20	/// Resolver helpers must consume what `init --repo-local` produces.
21	///
22	/// Builds a real dual-repo fixture (bare git remotes + colocated jj
23	/// repos + the canonical `.vc-config.toml` pair init writes), then
24	/// drives the resolver chain `sync()` uses when `-R` is empty:
25	///
26	/// - `find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude)` walks up to `fx.work`
27	///   (proves cwd-portability against init's `path = "/.claude"` /
28	///   `path = "/"` config split).
29	/// - `default_scope(Some(&fx.work))` reads the workspace config and
30	///   resolves to the dual-repo default.
31	/// - `scope_to_repos` maps each `Scope` shape to the right
32	///   absolute path(s) under the fixture.
33	///
34	/// Pure check on the resolver chain — does not invoke `sync()`
35	/// itself, since that walks `std::env::current_dir()` and parallel
36	/// `cargo test` makes cwd mutation unsafe.
37	#[test]
38	fn resolver_chain_against_init_repo_local() {
39	    let fx = Fixture::new("resolver-chain");
40	
41	    // Walk-up from the bot side lands on the app root.
42	    assert_eq!(
43	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude).as_deref(),
44	        Some(&*fx.work),
45	        "find_workspace_root should resolve from .claude up to work"
46	    );
47	    // Walk-up from the app root finds itself.
48	    assert_eq!(
49	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.work).as_deref(),
50	        Some(&*fx.work)
51	    );
52	
53	    // init --repo-local writes other-repo = ".claude", so the
54	    // workspace's default scope is dual.
55	    assert_eq!(
56	        default_scope(Some(&fx.work)),
57	        Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot])
58	    );
59	
60	    // Each scope shape resolves to the right absolute path(s).
61	    assert_eq!(
62	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
63	        vec![fx.work.clone(), fx.claude.clone()]
64	    );
65	    assert_eq!(
66	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
67	        vec![fx.work.clone()]
68	    );
69	    assert_eq!(
70	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
71	        vec![fx.claude.clone()]
72	    );
73	
74	    // sync_repos accepts the resolved list and reports up-to-date
75	    // — the resolver's output is shaped the way sync expects.
76	    let resolved = scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap();
77	    sync_repos(&resolved, &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed on resolved repos");
78	}
79	
80	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and assert success; returns trimmed stdout.
81	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
82	    let out = Command::new("jj")
83	        .args(args)
84	        .arg("-R")
85	        .arg(repo)
86	        .output()
87	        .expect("spawn jj");
88	    assert!(
89	        out.status.success(),
90	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
91	        repo.display(),
92	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
93	    );
94	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
95	}
96	
97	/// Resolve `rev` to its short commit id in `repo`.
98	fn cid(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
99	    jj(
100	        repo,
101	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
102	    )
103	}
104	
105	/// Sync params with `--no-check` set (apply mode).
106	///
107	/// Integration tests pass explicit repo paths through `sync_repos`
108	/// directly, so `repo` / `scope` stay `None` here and the CLI-side
109	/// default resolution is not exercised by this helper.
110	fn apply_params() -> SyncParams {
111	    SyncParams {
112	        no_check: true,
113	        quiet: false,
114	        bookmark: "main".to_string(),
115	        remote: "origin".to_string(),
116	        repo: None,
117	        scope: None,
118	    }
119	}
120	
121	/// Add a local-only commit on `main` in `repo` (not pushed), then
122	/// restore `@` to an empty child so pre-flight still passes.
123	///
124	/// Sequence: write file → describe `@` → advance `main` to `@` →
125	/// create a fresh empty `@` above it.
126	fn add_local_commit(repo: &Path, file: &str, content: &str, msg: &str) {
127	    fs::write(repo.join(file), content).expect("write local file");
128	    jj(repo, &["describe", "@", "-m", msg]);
129	    jj(repo, &["bookmark", "set", "main", "-r", "@"]);
130	    jj(repo, &["new"]);
131	}
132	
133	/// Clone `remote_url` into `<base>/<work_name>`, add a commit, push it.
134	///
135	/// Used to make the remote advance beyond the fixture's `main`
136	/// from a separate working copy. Returns the pushed commit's id.
137	fn push_from_clone(
138	    base: &Path,
139	    remote_url: &Path,
140	    work_name: &str,
141	    file: &str,
142	    content: &str,
143	    msg: &str,
144	) -> String {
145	    let workdir = base.join(work_name);
146	    let out = Command::new("jj")
147	        .args(["git", "clone", "--colocate"])
148	        .arg(remote_url)
149	        .arg(&workdir)
150	        .output()
151	        .expect("spawn jj clone");
152	    assert!(
153	        out.status.success(),
154	        "jj git clone failed: {}",
155	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
156	    );
157	    fs::write(workdir.join(file), content).expect("write remote file");
158	    jj(&workdir, &["describe", "@", "-m", msg]);
159	    jj(&workdir, &["bookmark", "set", "main", "-r", "@"]);
160	    jj(&workdir, &["git", "push", "--bookmark", "main"]);
161	    cid(&workdir, "main")
162	}
163	
164	/// Scenario 1: fresh fixture, nothing to do — `sync` leaves both
165	/// repos untouched.
166	#[test]
167	fn sync_up_to_date() {
168	    let fx = Fixture::new("up-to-date");
169	    let work_main = cid(&fx.work, "main");
170	    let claude_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
171	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
172	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.work, "main"), work_main);
173	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), claude_main);
174	}
175	
176	/// Scenario 2a: a non-empty `@` on top of main (simulates `/exit`
177	/// trailing session writes in `.claude`) is tolerated when there's
178	/// nothing new on the remote. `@`'s commit id changes because jj
179	/// snapshots the written file, but its content is preserved and
180	/// `@` stays reachable from main.
181	#[test]
182	fn sync_tolerates_trailing_at_up_to_date() {
183	    let fx = Fixture::new("trailing-uptodate");
184	    let pre_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
185	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl"), "{\"line\":1}\n").expect("write trailing file");
186	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
187	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), pre_main, "main should not move");
188	    let on_main = jj(
189	        &fx.claude,
190	        &[
191	            "log",
192	            "-r",
193	            "main::@",
194	            "--no-graph",
195	            "-T",
196	            r#"commit_id.short(12) ++ "\n""#,
197	        ],
198	    );
199	    assert!(!on_main.trim().is_empty(), "@ should stay on main's line");
200	    assert_eq!(
201	        fs::read_to_string(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl")).unwrap(),
202	        "{\"line\":1}\n",
203	        "trailing content preserved"
204	    );
205	}
206

208	/// while the session was offline. jj's fetch auto-ff's main but
209	/// leaves `@` behind; sync must then rebase `@` onto the new main

content.text:
Pre-commit cycle clean: fmt, clippy, 357 unit + 14 integration tests pass, binary at `0.54.0-2`.

`0.54.0-2` is substantively done — `Scope` relocated + collapsed, `sync -R` added, all 14 consumer files migrated. Proposed commit:

**Title** (48 chars):
```
refactor: relocate Scope, add sync -R (0.54.0-2)
```

**Body**:
```
Second substep of the scope CLI cleanup. `src/scope.rs` moves
into `options_flags/scope.rs`; `Scope` collapses from a
two-variant enum to a newtype over `Vec<Side>` (the `Single`
path variant is gone); `vc-x1 sync` gains `-R/--repo`. Design
narrative in chores-11.

- src/scope.rs: deleted (moved to options_flags/).
- src/options_flags/scope.rs: new — roles-only `Scope`
  newtype, `Side`, `parse_scope`. `Single`,
  `parse_scope_roles`, unused `Display` dropped.
- src/options_flags/mod.rs: `pub mod scope;` added.
- src/main.rs: `mod scope;` removed.
- src/common.rs: `default_scope` drops `cwd` param (POR →
  `Scope([Code])`); `scope_to_repos` drops the `Single` arm.
- src/sync.rs: `-R/--repo` flag added; `resolve_params_to_repos`
  becomes the four-case `(repo, scope)` compose.
- src/options_flags/common_args.rs: `parse_scope_roles` →
  `parse_scope`.
- src/init.rs: `Scope::Roles(v)` → `Scope(v)`; import.
- src/chid.rs, desc.rs, list.rs, show.rs: test imports +
  `Scope::Roles` → `Scope`.
- src/common/tests.rs, sync/tests.rs, sync/integration_tests.rs:
  tests follow the newtype + dropped `cwd` param; sync's
  path-form test → `-R` test.
- Cargo.toml: 0.54.0-1 → 0.54.0-2.
- notes/chores/chores-11.md: 0.54.0-1 `Commits:` backfill;
  new 0.54.0-2 section.
- notes/todo.md: 0.54.0-2 ladder flip.
```

OK to push?

message.content: Let's not delete the script fix-todo.py until we git our fix-todo subcommand written and let's write validate-todo first so we can validate against fix-todo.py, thoughts?

content.text:
Both good calls.

**Keeping `fix-todo.py` until the Rust `fix-todo` lands** — agreed. Deleting a working tool before its replacement exists leaves a real gap (the manual renumber convention still needs *something* to enforce it). So `0.54.0-3` becomes a pure docs/script sweep — README, ARCHITECTURE, substep-test.sh, notes refs to the old `--scope code,bot|por` form — and drops the "delete fix-todo.py" line.

**`validate-todo` first, as a differential test against `fix-todo.py`** — also sound. The setup: `fix-todo.py` *produces* canonical numbering; `validate-todo` *checks* for it. So you get a free oracle — run `fix-todo.py` on any todo.md, then `validate-todo` must pass on the result; run `validate-todo` on a pre-fix todo.md and it must report exactly the drift `fix-todo.py` would correct. Clean.

One subtlety worth noting: the oracle only holds if `validate-todo` and `fix-todo.py` agree on what "canonical" means. `fix-todo.py` defines it operationally (its output); `validate-todo` defines it declaratively (a checker). Any divergence makes the oracle lie — but that's exactly what the differential test surfaces, and you reconcile during development. Build order `validate-todo` → `fix-todo` is the right call: the checker is the spec, the fixer implements to it.

**Impact, concretely:**
- `0.54.0-3` (this cycle): pure docs/script sweep, no deletion. I'll correct the `todo.md > ## In Progress` ladder line ("+ delete `notes/fix-todo.py`" → just "docs/script sweep") when `0.54.0-3` starts. The committed `0.54.0-0` opener-section ladder stays as-is — the close-out's as-built ladder records what actually happened.
- **todo #1** entry should capture both decisions: keep `fix-todo.py` until the Rust `fix-todo` exists, and build `validate-todo` first as the spec. Worth folding into `0.54.0-3` (it's a notes edit, fits the sweep) or doing now.

Want me to update todo #1 now, or roll it into `0.54.0-3`?

message.content:
The term "sweep" is nebulous to me but it implied it might mean deleting,
which is why I mentioned it. So define what you mean when you say "sweep"?

content.text:
"Sweep" = a systematic pass over a defined set of files, applying **one consistent kind of update** to bring them in line with a change already made elsewhere. It is an *update/correct* operation — find stale references, fix them. Not deletion, not new features.

Concretely for `0.54.0-3`'s "docs/script sweep": substeps `-1` and `-2` changed the scope CLI surface (`--por` replaced `--scope=code,bot|por`, `--scope` is now roles-only, `sync` gained `-R`, `options_flags/scope.rs` was deleted). The **code and tests** were updated as part of those substeps. But **prose and scripts** still describe the old surface. The sweep walks those and corrects them. Targets:

- `README.md` — usage examples / flag descriptions mentioning `--scope code,bot|por` or path-in-scope.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — any scope description.
- `notes/substep-test.sh` — if it invokes `--scope` in a now-invalid form.
- `notes/*.md` design notes — references to the old scope surface.
- `CLAUDE.md` — if it mentions scope flags.

Output: those files updated to describe the *current* surface. Nothing removed.

Your instinct wasn't wrong, though — the ladder line read `0.54.0-3 docs/script sweep + delete notes/fix-todo.py`. The `delete` was a **separate item appended with `+`**, not part of "sweep" itself. Now that we've dropped the deletion, `0.54.0-3` is purely a sweep: updates only.

If "sweep" stays nebulous, I'm happy to rename the ladder/chores wording to something concrete like "docs/script update for the new scope surface" — clearer, no jargon. Want that?

content.content:
115	```
116	
117	**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
118	(just the working copy).
119	
120	Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, `-R`/`--repo`, and
121	`-s`/`--scope` take precedence over positional arguments.
122	
123	### Multi-repo queries
124	
125	`-s`/`--scope` selects workspace sides by role keyword; `code` is the
126	app repo, `bot` is the `.claude` repo, `code,bot` is both. The
127	workspace root is found by walking up from cwd (the existing
128	`find_workspace_root` rule).
129	
130	```
131	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot          # both sides of the workspace
132	vc-x1 chid -s code              # just the app repo
133	vc-x1 chid -s bot               # just the bot repo
134	vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot    # works with chid / desc / list / show
135	```
136	
137	`-R`/`--repo` takes a single path. Used alone it points at one jj
138	repo (workspace lookup is skipped); combined with `-s` it overrides
139	the workspace root for the role lookup:
140	
141	```
142	vc-x1 chid -R .                  # single repo at .
143	vc-x1 chid -R .claude            # single repo at .claude
144	vc-x1 chid -R ../other -s code,bot   # both sides of ../other workspace
145	```
146	
147	Defaults preserve prior behavior — no flag → `[.]`, `-R foo` alone
148	→ `[foo]`. When multiple repos are resolved (any `-s` that names
149	more than one role), output is labeled with bold `=== path ===`
150	headers by default. Control the label with `-l`/`--label` and
151	`-L`/`--no-label`:
152	
153	```
154	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot            # default label: === path ===
155	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -l "---"   # custom label:  --- path ---
156	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -L         # no label (raw output)
157	```
158	
159	Examples:
160	
161	```
162	$ vc-x1 chid -s code,bot
163	=== . ===
164	kwoyvposvsmv
165	
166	=== .claude ===
167	zzpksklxyrnw
168	
169	$ vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -L
170	kwoyvposvsmv
171	zzpksklxyrnw
172	
173	$ vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot
174	=== . ===
175	kwoyvposvsmv eae175c3c1b5 (no description set)
176	pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
177	ssokyszzwsxw eeb15bc6d839 Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)
178	
179	=== .claude ===
180	zzpksklxyrnw 2388185e42ee (no description set)
181	tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
182	slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)
183	
184	$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -s code,bot
185	=== . ===
186	pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)
187	
188	    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
189	    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.
190	
191	    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp
192	
193	=== .claude ===
194	tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)
195	
196	    Session: reviewed and committed notes reorganization for 0.19.1.
197	
198	    ochid: pqxtxxpnsmot
199	
200	$ vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -l "---"
201	--- . ---
202	kwoyvposvsmv
203	
204	--- .claude ---
205	zzpksklxyrnw
206	```
207	
208	Most decoration strings work unquoted (`---`, `===`, `>>>`, `:::`,
209	`+++`). Use single quotes for strings containing shell metacharacters
210	like `*`, `!`, `#`, `$`, or `~` (e.g. `-l '***'`). Double quotes
211	won't protect against `!` (bash history expansion).
212	
213	With a single repo resolved, no label is printed — backward
214	compatible with the no-flag default. Multi-repo (`-s code,bot`) is
215	supported for `chid`, `desc`, `list`, and `show`. `finalize` remains
216	single-repo.
217	
218	`-s` is keyword-only today. Path-via-`-s` and a comma-list form
219	mixing one path with role keywords (e.g. `-s ../foo,code,bot` as a
220	workspace-root-plus-roles shorthand) are planned — see
221	[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).
222	
223	### validate-desc
224	
225	Read-only scan of commit descriptions against the other repo. The other
226	repo is read from `.vc-config.toml` (`workspace.other-repo`) by default,
227	or overridden with `--other-repo`. Reports status per commit: `ok`
228	(valid ochid), `lost` (ochid: lost), `none` (ochid: none), `err`
229	(issues found), or `miss` (no ochid trailer).

```

**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
(just the working copy).

Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, `-R`/`--repo`, and
`-s`/`--scope` take precedence over positional arguments.

### Multi-repo queries

`-s`/`--scope` selects workspace sides by role keyword; `code` is the
app repo, `bot` is the `.claude` repo, `code,bot` is both. The
workspace root is found by walking up from cwd (the existing
`find_workspace_root` rule).

```
vc-x1 chid -s code,bot          # both sides of the workspace
vc-x1 chid -s code              # just the app repo
vc-x1 chid -s bot               # just the bot repo
vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot    # works with chid / desc / list / show
```

`-R`/`--repo` takes a single path. Used alone it points at one jj
repo (workspace lookup is skipped); combined with `-s` it overrides
the workspace root for the role lookup:

```
vc-x1 chid -R .                  # single repo at .
vc-x1 chid -R .claude            # single repo at .claude
vc-x1 chid -R ../other -s code,bot   # both sides of ../other workspace
```

Defaults preserve prior behavior — no flag → `[.]`, `-R foo` alone
→ `[foo]`. When multiple repos are resolved (any `-s` that names
more than one role), output is labeled with bold `=== path ===`
headers by default. Control the label with `-l`/`--label` and
`-L`/`--no-label`:

```
vc-x1 chid -s code,bot            # default label: === path ===
vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -l "---"   # custom label:  --- path ---
vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -L         # no label (raw output)
```

Examples:

```
$ vc-x1 chid -s code,bot
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv eae175c3c1b5 (no description set)
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
ssokyszzwsxw eeb15bc6d839 Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw 2388185e42ee (no description set)
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -s code,bot
=== . ===
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.

    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp

=== .claude ===
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Session: reviewed and committed notes reorganization for 0.19.1.

    ochid: pqxtxxpnsmot

$ vc-x1 chid -s code,bot -l "---"
--- . ---
kwoyvposvsmv

--- .claude ---
zzpksklxyrnw
```

Most decoration strings work unquoted (`---`, `===`, `>>>`, `:::`,
`+++`). Use single quotes for strings containing shell metacharacters
like `*`, `!`, `#`, `$`, or `~` (e.g. `-l '***'`). Double quotes
won't protect against `!` (bash history expansion).

With a single repo resolved, no label is printed — backward
compatible with the no-flag default. Multi-repo (`-s code,bot`) is
supported for `chid`, `desc`, `list`, and `show`. `finalize` remains
single-repo.

`-s` is keyword-only today. Path-via-`-s` and a comma-list form
mixing one path with role keywords (e.g. `-s ../foo,code,bot` as a
workspace-root-plus-roles shorthand) are planned — see
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

### validate-desc

Read-only scan of commit descriptions against the other repo. The other
repo is read from `.vc-config.toml` (`workspace.other-repo`) by default,
or overridden with `--other-repo`. Reports status per commit: `ok`
(valid ochid), `lost` (ochid: lost), `none` (ochid: none), `err`
(issues found), or `miss` (no ochid trailer).

85	
86	## Module map
87	
88	The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
89	catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
90	per-subcommand refactor status):
91	
92	**CLI layer:**
93	
94	- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
95	  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
96	  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
97	  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
98	  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
99	- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
100	  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
101	  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
102	  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
103	  exactly once. See
104	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).

file.content:
## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand refactor status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).

389	### sync
390	
391	Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes in a single command.
392	Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
393	`.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo projects
394	or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces. Dry-run by default — re-run with
395	`--no-dry-run` to apply.
396	
397	Per repo, `sync` classifies the local bookmark against its remote:
398	
399	| State | Meaning | Action on `--no-dry-run` |
400	|------|---------|--------------------------|
401	| up-to-date | local == remote | none |
402	| behind | local is ancestor of remote | `jj bookmark set <b> -r <b>@<remote>` |
403	| ahead | remote is ancestor of local | none (push is a separate step) |
404	| diverged | neither is ancestor | `jj rebase -b <local-head> -d <b>@<remote>` |
405	| no remote | bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart | none — skip |
406	
407	After the bookmark action above, `sync` also rebases `@` onto the
408	(possibly advanced) bookmark when `@` isn't already a descendant —
409	without this step, `jj git fetch`'s auto-fast-forward would leave `@`
410	dangling off the pre-fetch bookmark commit. This matters for `.claude`,
411	where `/exit`'s trailing session writes always sit on `@`.
412	
413	On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
414	restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
415	every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
416	across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
417	content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
418	rebase are abandoned on the way back.
419	
420	```
421	vc-x1 sync                            # workspace-default scope, --check
422	vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
423	vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # workspace-default scope, apply
424	vc-x1 sync --scope=code               # only the app repo
425	vc-x1 sync --scope=bot                # only the bot repo
426	vc-x1 sync --scope=code,bot           # both (explicit form of the dual default)
427	vc-x1 sync -R .                       # arbitrary repo set (no workspace lookup)
428	vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
429	```
430	
431	Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
432	explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
433	explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
434	default.
435	
436	**Repo set resolution.** Sync picks the repo list in this order:
437	
438	1. `-R` / `--repo` — exactly that list.
439	2. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles, resolved via the
440	   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml` (`code` → root,
441	   `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
442	3. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
443	   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
444	   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
445	
446	`-R` and `--scope` are mutually exclusive — they answer different
447	questions ("arbitrary repo set" vs "workspace roles"). Scope is
448	cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to the
449	workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.
450	
451	| Flag | Description |
452	|------|-------------|
453	| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated. Mutually exclusive with `--scope` |
454	| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Mutually exclusive with `-R` |
455	| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
456	| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
457	| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
458	| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
459	| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |
460	
461	**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:

file.content:
### sync

Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes in a single command.
Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
`.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo projects
or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces. Dry-run by default — re-run with
`--no-dry-run` to apply.

Per repo, `sync` classifies the local bookmark against its remote:

| State | Meaning | Action on `--no-dry-run` |
|------|---------|--------------------------|
| up-to-date | local == remote | none |
| behind | local is ancestor of remote | `jj bookmark set <b> -r <b>@<remote>` |
| ahead | remote is ancestor of local | none (push is a separate step) |
| diverged | neither is ancestor | `jj rebase -b <local-head> -d <b>@<remote>` |
| no remote | bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart | none — skip |

After the bookmark action above, `sync` also rebases `@` onto the
(possibly advanced) bookmark when `@` isn't already a descendant —
without this step, `jj git fetch`'s auto-fast-forward would leave `@`
dangling off the pre-fetch bookmark commit. This matters for `.claude`,
where `/exit`'s trailing session writes always sit on `@`.

On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
rebase are abandoned on the way back.

```
vc-x1 sync                            # workspace-default scope, --check
vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # workspace-default scope, apply
vc-x1 sync --scope=code               # only the app repo
vc-x1 sync --scope=bot                # only the bot repo
vc-x1 sync --scope=code,bot           # both (explicit form of the dual default)
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # arbitrary repo set (no workspace lookup)
vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
```

Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
default.

**Repo set resolution.** Sync picks the repo list in this order:

1. `-R` / `--repo` — exactly that list.
2. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles, resolved via the
   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml` (`code` → root,
   `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
3. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.

`-R` and `--scope` are mutually exclusive — they answer different
questions ("arbitrary repo set" vs "workspace roles"). Scope is
cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to the
workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated. Mutually exclusive with `--scope` |
| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Mutually exclusive with `-R` |
| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |

**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:

- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:

aiTitle: Review options_flags and scope argument design

content.text:
Found a real conflict, not just staleness. The README `### sync` section already specs a sync `-R` flag — and it's a **different design** than what I implemented in `0.54.0-2`:

| Aspect | README `### sync` spec | What `0.54.0-2` implemented |
|---|---|---|
| `-R` arity | repeatable / comma-separated (`-R .,.claude -R ../other`) | single path (`Option<PathBuf>`) |
| `-R` vs `--scope` | **mutually exclusive** ("different questions") | **composes** (`-R PATH -s ROLES` → PATH as workspace root) |
| `-R` meaning | arbitrary repo set, no workspace lookup | workspace-root override, mirrors `CommonArgs`'s `-R` |

The README sync section was written as a spec ahead of implementation — sync never actually had `-R` in code before `0.54.0-2`. I built `-R` to match `CommonArgs`'s pattern (single path, composes) without checking that the README had already spec'd it differently. That's a miss on my part — I should have read the README sync section during `0.54.0-2`.

This isn't something the "update docs" sweep can resolve mechanically — it's a design fork:

- **(A) Keep `0.54.0-2`'s implementation**, rewrite the README sync section to match (single-path `-R`, composes with `-s`). Cost: the README's "arbitrary repeatable repo set" capability is dropped; sync's `-R` stays consistent with every other command's `-R`.
- **(B) Adopt the README's spec**, revise the sync code (repeatable `-R`, mutually exclusive with `-s`). Cost: a code change (could fold into `0.54.0-3` or be a new substep); sync's `-R` then behaves unlike `CommonArgs`'s `-R`.

The bot leans **(A)** — one consistent `-R` across the CLI beats a bespoke sync-only arity, and the migration the cycle needed ("sync one repo by path", replacing `--scope <path>`) is fully covered by single-path `-R`. The README's repeatable-set idea could become its own todo if it's still wanted.

Separately, the README sync section has unrelated pre-existing rot: line 394 says "Dry-run by default — re-run with `--no-dry-run`", but sync uses `--check`/`--no-check` (the rest of the section gets this right). I'll fix that regardless.

Which `-R` design — (A) or (B)?

message.content: (A) as we have it now. Note, I added a Note to #2

file.content:
## 1. Parity

por and dual should be first-class equals across every
subcommand — same code paths, same quality of support, no
"dual plus an afterthought" asymmetry. The `--scope`
rollout (`code,bot` vs `por` — see `notes/todo.md` and
`src/options_flags/scope.rs`'s `ScopeKind`) is the
substrate; parity is the goal that rollout serves.

## 2. Bidirectional conversion

A new capability: convert a workspace between the two
shapes in place.

- **`por → dual`** — attach a `.claude` companion repo and
  a `.vc-config.toml` to an existing single repo; emit
  cross-links going forward.
- **`dual → por`** — detach the `.claude` companion; drop
  `.vc-config.toml`; the code repo continues standalone.

## Open questions (resolve at design time)

- Surface: a new subcommand (`vc-x1 convert`?
  `promote` / `demote`?) vs. flags on existing commands.
- `dual → por` — what happens to the `.claude` history:
  archived, left in place untouched, deleted? Leading
  guess is "left alone, just unlinked," but TBD.
- `.vc-config.toml` — creation on `por → dual` (which
  fields, defaults from user config?) and removal on
  `dual → por`.
- Whether `init` / `clone` change, or conversion is purely
  a separate operation on an already-existing workspace.
- Idempotency / re-running; recovery from a conversion
  interrupted mid-way.

## See also

- `notes/todo.md` — the por/dual item, and the `--scope`
  cluster it builds on.
- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `ScopeKind` (`CodeBot` /
  `Por`) and `parse_scope_kind`.
- `notes/chores/chores-07.md > ## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)`

input.old_string:
**Repo set resolution.** Sync picks the repo list in this order:

1. `-R` / `--repo` — exactly that list.
2. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles, resolved via the
   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml` (`code` → root,
   `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
3. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.

`-R` and `--scope` are mutually exclusive — they answer different
questions ("arbitrary repo set" vs "workspace roles"). Scope is
cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to the
workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated. Mutually exclusive with `--scope` |
| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Mutually exclusive with `-R` |
input.new_string:
**Repo set resolution.** `-R` and `--scope` compose:

1. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
2. `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at `PATH`.
3. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` alone — workspace roles, resolved
   via the discovered workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`
   (`code` → root, `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
4. `-R PATH --scope=ROLES` — roles resolved against `PATH` as the
   workspace root.

Scope is cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to
the workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Workspace root, or a single repo to sync alone. Composes with `--scope` |
| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Composes with `-R` |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores/chores-*.md`
and `notes/todo.md`.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand refactor status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and the
  `-R`/`-s` resolution stack — `find_workspace_root` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` / `resolve_repos`
  (the top-level entry combining the `-R PATH` override and
  the `-s code|bot|code,bot` role selection).

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for explicit
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` and which
compose `options_flags/` leaves: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` +
`notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## args → Context + Params

"The Context+Params port" — each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` is structured as
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, with an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0)
is the worked example; complete across all 12 current
subcommands as of 0.49.0, and the shape new subcommands
follow. Out of scope until a real consumer surfaces: typed
errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`,
`Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand
status: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` (the "Refactor tracking" section).

## per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of the Context+Params port — "the options_flags
extraction" — per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` fields are being
lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under `src/options_flags/`
so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for the options_flags extraction.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both refactors.
- `notes/chores/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  refactor-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).

# por/dual parity + bidirectional conversion

Forward-looking design capture (pre-design). Concrete work
lands when a cycle picks it up — likely on top of the
`--scope` rollout (the `0.50.0` work in `notes/todo.md`).

`vc-x1` today treats the **dual** workspace (a code repo
plus a `.claude` bot-session repo, cross-linked by `ochid:`
trailers, configured by `.vc-config.toml`) as the primary
shape; **por** (Plain Old Repo — a single repo, no
`.claude/`, no `.vc-config.toml`) is supported but bolted
on. Two goals:

## 1. Parity

por and dual should be first-class equals across every
subcommand — same code paths, same quality of support, no
"dual plus an afterthought" asymmetry. The `--scope`
rollout (`code,bot` vs `por` — see `notes/todo.md` and
`src/options_flags/scope.rs`'s `ScopeKind`) is the
substrate; parity is the goal that rollout serves.

## 2. Bidirectional conversion

A new capability: convert a workspace between the two
shapes in place.

- **`por → dual`** — attach a `.claude` companion repo and
  a `.vc-config.toml` to an existing single repo; emit
  cross-links going forward.
- **`dual → por`** — detach the `.claude` companion; drop
  `.vc-config.toml`; the code repo continues standalone.

## Open questions (resolve at design time)

- Surface: a new subcommand (`vc-x1 convert`?
  `promote` / `demote`?) vs. flags on existing commands.
- `dual → por` — what happens to the `.claude` history:
  archived, left in place untouched, deleted? Leading
  guess is "left alone, just unlinked," but TBD.
- `.vc-config.toml` — creation on `por → dual` (which
  fields, defaults from user config?) and removal on
  `dual → por`.
- Whether `init` / `clone` change, or conversion is purely
  a separate operation on an already-existing workspace.
- Idempotency / re-running; recovery from a conversion
  interrupted mid-way.

## See also

- `notes/todo.md` — the por/dual item, and the `--scope`
  cluster it builds on.
- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `ScopeKind` (`CodeBot` /
  `Por`) and `parse_scope_kind`.
- `notes/chores/chores-07.md > ## init + clone redesign (0.41.1)`
  — the `init_one` / `clone_one` primitives that aim to
  give a single chokepoint for scope-shaped changes.

lines: por and dual should be first-class equals across every
subcommand — same code paths, same quality of support, no
-substrate; parity is the goal that rollout serves.
+"dual plus an afterthought" asymmetry. The `--por` topology
+substrate; parity is the goal it serves.

  topology flag.

  guess is "left alone, just unlinked," but TBD.

## docs: update for new scope surface (0.54.0-3)

Third substep. Substeps -1 and -2 changed the scope CLI
surface (code + tests updated as they went); this substep
brings the prose and two design notes in line.

- README.md `### sync` spec'd a `-R` flag the code never
  had — repeatable / comma-separated, mutually exclusive
  with `--scope`. `0.54.0-2` implemented `-R` differently:
  single path, composes with `--scope`, mirroring
  `CommonArgs`'s `-R`. The user chose to keep the
  as-built design (one consistent `-R` across the CLI) and
  the section is rewritten to match. The intro and the
  state-table header also carried pre-existing rot —
  `--dry-run` / `--no-dry-run` where sync actually uses
  `--check` / `--no-check`; fixed.
- README.md `### Multi-repo queries` dropped the stale
  "path-via-`-s` planned" paragraph — paths route through
  `-R`, never a future `-s` form.
- ARCHITECTURE.md: the `src/scope.rs` module bullet became
  `src/options_flags/scope.rs`, `Scope` described as the
  roles-only newtype.
- `notes/por-dual-parity.md` / `notes/bot-data-formats.md`:
  dangling references to the deleted `ScopeKind` /
  `Scope::Single` re-pointed at `--por` / `PorFlag`.

`notes/fix-todo.py` is intentionally **not** deleted — it
stays until the Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` subcommand (todo #1)
replaces it. The cycle-opener ladder said "delete
`notes/fix-todo.py`"; that was dropped mid-cycle.

file.content:
writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

input.new_string:
`notes/fix-todo.py` is intentionally **not** deleted — it
stays until the Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` subcommand (todo #1)
replaces it. The cycle-opener ladder said "delete
`notes/fix-todo.py`"; that was dropped mid-cycle.

## refactor: scope CLI cleanup (0.54.0)

Cycle close-out. The two `--scope` flags that opened the
cycle — query-side roles and creation-side topology — are
now three orthogonal flags: `-R/--repo` (path),
`-s/--scope` (roles, keyword-only), `--por` (topology).
One `scope` module, one `Scope` type (a roles-only
newtype), no `ScopeKind`.

### As-built ladder

- 0.54.0-0 plan + version bump + chores opener
- 0.54.0-1 `--por` boolean flag leaf replaces
  `options_flags/scope.rs` (`ScopeKind`); init / clone /
  test_helpers migrated
- 0.54.0-2 `Scope` collapsed to a roles-only newtype and
  relocated to `options_flags/scope.rs`;
  `parse_scope_roles` dropped; `sync` gained `-R/--repo`
- 0.54.0-3 docs updated for the new surface
- 0.54.0 close-out

### Outcome

Four substeps after the opener, no evaluation-gate detours
or abandonments. Two shape changes emerged mid-cycle:

- The substep order swapped (delete `options_flags/scope`
  in -1 before collapsing `src/scope.rs` in -2) so only
  one `scope` module existed during the collapse — the
  user flagged the dual-`scope` confusion at cycle start.
- `0.54.0-3` surfaced that README's `### sync` had spec'd
  a `-R` the code never had (repeatable, mutually
  exclusive with `--scope`); `0.54.0-2` had implemented a
  different `-R` (single path, composes). The user chose
  the as-built design and the docs were rewritten to
  match — the repeatable-set idea is unclaimed if anyone
  wants it later.

`notes/fix-todo.py` was kept rather than deleted (the
opener ladder said "delete"; dropped mid-cycle) — it
stays until the Rust `vc-x1 fix-todo` subcommand (todo
#1) replaces it.

# References

content.description: Cycle opener for scope CLI cleanup. Backfill 0.53.0 Commits ref, bump Cargo 0.53.0 → 0.54.0-0, new chores section with three-orthogonal-concerns design + substep ladder, todo In Progress ladder populated, pre-commit cycle, push.

content.subject: 0.54.0-1: delete options_flags/scope.rs + --por flag
content.description: Delete `options_flags/scope.rs` (ScopeKind, ScopeOption, parse_scope_kind, ScopeParser). Add a PorFlag boolean leaf in `options_flags/`. Migrate init, clone, test_helpers from ScopeKind/ScopeOption to the new --por flag. Help text + tests.

content.subject: 0.54.0-2: collapse Scope + relocate + sync -R
content.description: Drop Scope::Single(PathBuf); make Scope a newtype `pub struct Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`. Drop parse_scope_roles. Relocate src/scope.rs into options_flags/scope.rs and update all imports `crate::scope::*` → `crate::options_flags::scope::*`. POR default_scope returns Roles([Code]). Add `-R/--repo` to sync. Update CommonArgs help text. Tests.

content.subject: 0.54.0-3: update docs/script for new scope surface
content.description: Update README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, notes/substep-test.sh, CLAUDE.md, and notes/*.md design notes that still describe the old scope CLI surface (`--scope code,bot|por`, path-in-scope). Bring them in line with the new surface (`--por` flag, roles-only `--scope`, sync `-R`). No deletions — `notes/fix-todo.py` stays until the Rust fix-todo subcommand exists.

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` below begin every
7	line with exactly 1 leading space so they don't accidentally match
8	the renumber script's entry regex (`^\d+\.\s`). Minimum is the
9	principled choice — the convention's job is to dodge the
10	column-0 anchor, not to add visual emphasis. See
11	`notes/fix-todo.py`.
12	
13	## In Progress
14	
15	A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
16	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
17	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
18	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
19	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
20	
21	_No cycle currently in progress._
22	
23	## Todo
24	
25	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
26	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
27	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
28	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
29	
30	 Entries are manually numbered (`1.` `2.` …) so the index is
31	 readable in the raw markdown source. Reorder/insert renumbers the
32	 entries below — accept the diff churn in exchange for being
33	 able to count without rendering. Reference by entry number
34	 ("let's work on #3"). Continuation lines under a two-digit
35	 entry indent 4 spaces (under `NN. `) rather than 3.
36	
37	1. **`vc-x1 fix-todo` / `validate-todo` subcommands.** Parallel
38	   shape to `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` (check vs rewrite).
39	   Renumber `## Todo` and `## Bugs` entries in `notes/todo.md`,
40	   normalize continuation-line indent to match prefix width.
41	   Rust replacement for the interim `notes/fix-todo.py`.
42	   Belongs in the broader "notes/repo hygiene" wave alongside
43	   `validate-repo` ([[19]]).
44	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
45	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
46	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
47	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
48	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
49	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
50	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
51	   (`init.rs`, tests).
52	
53	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
54	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
55	   issue.
56	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
57	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
58	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
59	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
60	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
61	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
62	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
63	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
64	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
65	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
66	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
67	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
68	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
69	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
70	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
71	   the felt bottleneck.
72	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
73	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
74	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
75	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
76	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
77	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
78	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
79	6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
80	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
81	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
82	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
83	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
84	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
85	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
86	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
87	7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
88	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
89	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
90	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
91	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
92	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
93	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
94	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
95	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
96	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
97	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
98	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
99	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
100	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
101	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
102	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
103	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
104	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
105	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
106	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
107	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
108	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
109	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
110	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
111	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
112	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
113	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
114	    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
115	    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
116	    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
117	    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
118	11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
119	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
